articles.headline: Indian markets end FY26 with worst annual performance since pandemic
articles.summary: Indian benchmark indices logged their worst annual performance in six years as FY26 concluded. The Nifty 50 fell 5% and the Sensex declined 7% due to the West Asia war, a weakening rupee, and foreign fund outflows. Foreign institutional investors net sold equities worth ₹1.12 trillion in March alone, contrasting with earlier gains as geopolitical tensions weighed on global sentiment.

articles.headline: RBI caps bank net open forex positions at $100 million
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has capped banks’ net open positions in the domestic forex market at $100 million to support the rupee and curb speculative bets. Effective April 10, the new rule forces lenders to unwind large dollar positions, potentially leading to mark-to-market losses. The move marks a departure from previous limits allowed up to 25% of capital to manage exchange rate volatility.

articles.headline: Indian rupee records worst annual decline in 14 years during FY26
articles.summary: The rupee depreciated 10.8% in FY26, its worst performance since FY12, driven by energy shocks and capital outflows. Despite hitting record lows near 95 per dollar, the RBI has implemented strict caps on bank dollar positions to manage volatility. Economists suggest the central bank should prioritize preserving its $700 billion forex war chest over aggressive currency defense if war persists.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha passes bill to fast-track corporate debt resolution under IBC
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha approved amendments to the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) to introduce a creditor-initiated resolution process. The bill allows for fast-track resolution without unseating management and introduces frameworks for group and cross-border insolvency. Since its inception, the IBC has helped banks recover ₹54,528 crore of non-performing assets from 1,376 resolved companies.

articles.headline: Karnataka reintroduces tax on electric vehicles eroding price competitiveness
articles.summary: A new bill passed in the Karnataka assembly imposes a lifetime tax of 5-10% on electric cars, reversing a previous waiver. EVs priced below ₹10 lakh will attract a 5% tax, while those above ₹25 lakh will be taxed at 10%. Industry experts warn the move may slow adoption in a key market where EVs previously enjoyed a competitive edge over internal combustion engine vehicles.

articles.headline: RBI injects ₹84,582 crore liquidity through variable rate repo auctions
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India injected ₹84,582 crore of transient liquidity into the banking system via two variable rate repo (VRR) auctions on Monday. The first three-day auction saw ₹50,001 crore injected at a weighted average rate of 5.44%. The move aims to manage short-term liquidity mismatches within the Indian banking sector as the financial year concludes.

articles.headline: Bank credit to Indian industry grows 13.5% in February
articles.summary: RBI data shows bank credit to industry grew at 13.5% in the fortnight ending February 28, significantly higher than the 7.5% growth recorded a year ago. The surge was driven by demand in infrastructure, engineering, chemicals, and textiles. Growth in petroleum and nuclear fuel sectors also contributed to the buoyant industrial credit environment.

articles.headline: Aviation fuel supplies sufficient for 60 days despite regional war
articles.summary: Civil Aviation Minister Kinjarapu Rammohan Naidu informed the Rajya Sabha that India maintains a 60-day supply of aviation turbine fuel (ATF). The statement sought to reassure the industry amid concerns over potential fuel shortages caused by the escalating West Asia oil crisis. The government confirmed that current stock levels are adequate to prevent any immediate flight interruptions.

articles.headline: India's rapeseed-mustard production projected to reach 11.94 million tonnes
articles.summary: The Solvent Extractors’ Association of India (SEA) estimates rapeseed-mustard production will rise to 11.94 million tonnes in the 2025-26 rabi season, up from 11.52 million tonnes last year. The estimate, based on field surveys and remote sensing, suggests improved domestic oilseed availability despite broader global agricultural supply chain pressures.

articles.headline: Manufacturing sector drives February industrial production growth to 5.2%
articles.summary: India's industrial production growth accelerated to 5.2% in February, led by a 6.0% expansion in manufacturing. High-performing sectors included basic metals and motor vehicles. While capital goods output rose 12.5%, consumer non-durables contracted by 0.6%, indicating mixed recovery in mass consumption. Electricity generation and mining also posted moderate gains during the month.

articles.headline: Fuel excise duty cuts to lower oil company under-recoveries
articles.summary: The government's ₹10-per-litre excise duty cut on petrol and diesel has shifted the breakeven crude price for oil marketing companies (OMCs) to approximately $106 per barrel. While the move will cost the exchequer ₹14,000 crore monthly, it provides earnings visibility for firms like IOCL, BPCL, and HPCL. However, under-recoveries remain a risk if Brent crude stays above $115.

articles.headline: FPI outflows from Indian equities hit 34-year high in FY26
articles.summary: Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) pulled out nearly ₹1.8 trillion from Indian equities in FY26, the highest since 1992. The exodus was triggered by US-led tariff concerns and the US-Israel-Iran war. March 2026 alone saw a sell-off of ₹1.18 trillion. Higher global yields and slowing earnings growth have dampened India's relative appeal despite its strong macroeconomic fundamentals.

articles.headline: Small-cap funds see recovery in inflows following January record redemptions
articles.summary: Small-cap mutual funds saw inflows of ₹3,881 crore in February, rebounding from record redemptions in January. Despite volatility and liquidity risks, fund houses are launching new schemes to meet renewed investor interest. Experts warn that low liquidity and stretched valuations remain dangers, though domestic investors continue to hunt for value as foreign investors exit large caps.

articles.headline: Frictionless investing and stable returns drive Indian retail market surge
articles.summary: Higher awareness, digitalization, and stable long-term returns have propelled retail participation in India's capital markets. Despite recent volatility, 56% of investors now hold assets for more than two years. Equity assets under management have reached ₹36 trillion. Frictionless investing platforms have been cited as a game changer in shifting retail mindsets toward long-term wealth creation.

articles.headline: Precious metals rebound as gold and silver prices rise in Delhi
articles.summary: Gold prices rose 2.5% to ₹1.51 lakh per 10gm, while silver jumped 3% to ₹2.37 lakh per kg in Delhi on Monday. The rebound was driven by jewellers' buying ahead of the wedding season and a reversal in the rupee. Despite the daily gain, gold has lost 12.3% and silver 21% since early March due to geopolitical volatility and fluctuating US Treasury bond yields.

articles.headline: Analysis of India's flexible inflation targeting framework renewal for 2026-31
articles.summary: The government's decision to renew the 4% inflation target and 2-6% tolerance band for another five years provides stability amid global uncertainty. However, experts suggest that missing the opportunity to broaden the RBI's mandate or mandate public policy reporting remains a disappointment. The status quo avoids rocking the boat as the West Asia war continues to strain macroeconomics.

articles.headline: WTO fails to extend moratorium on e-commerce customs duties
articles.summary: WTO members failed to reach a consensus on extending the moratorium on customs duties for e-commerce at the Fourteenth Ministerial Conference. Consequently, countries like India and Brazil may now levy tariffs on electronic transmissions, including digital downloads and streaming. The moratorium had been in place since 1998 and was previously renewed every two years.

articles.headline: Delhi government promises no power tariff hike despite regulatory asset dues
articles.summary: Delhi Power Minister Ashish Sood announced that the government will not allow increased power tariffs to burden residents. This comes as the DERC deliberates on a plan to liquidate ₹38,000 crore in regulatory asset dues to distribution companies. The administration also plans to conduct a CAG audit of discoms to examine their financial positions following Supreme Court directions.

articles.headline: Analysis shows Muslims in Assam are overwhelmingly employed in agriculture
articles.summary: An HT analysis of the 2025 Periodic Labour Force Survey shows Muslims in Assam have a higher employment share in agriculture (41.8%) than Hindus, unlike the national trend. Only 10.7% of Muslim workers in the state have regular salaried jobs, compared to 21.5% for Hindus. The data suggests that Muslims in Assam often engage in farming due to limited access to quality salaried employment.

articles.headline: Karnataka issues guidelines for commercial LPG distribution amid supply shortage
articles.summary: Karnataka issued standard operating procedures for commercial LPG distribution due to a 40% supply deficit caused by the West Asian war. Essential institutions like hospitals and hostels will receive top priority. Minister K.H. Muniyappa also advised rural households to use dry firewood as an alternative while the government works to stabilize supply and curb black marketing.

articles.headline: South Korea considers public driving restrictions amid surging oil prices
articles.summary: Senior South Korean officials are considering public driving curbs to reduce energy demand as global oil prices soar due to the US-Israel-Iran war. The government is monitoring the situation to determine if mandatory restrictions are necessary. The move aims to rein in consumption as energy security concerns grow across Asia following supply disruptions in the Persian Gulf.

articles.headline: Nirmala Sitharaman defends Indian economy despite global currency volatility
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman told the Lok Sabha that India's economic fundamentals remain strong despite the rupee's slide. She refuting claims that the PM's credibility is falling, citing his high global approval ratings. Sitharaman highlighted growth above 7% and solid foreign exchange reserves as buffers against the volatility triggered by the West Asian conflict.

articles.headline: India's industrial production grew 5.2 percent in February
articles.summary: India's Index of Industrial Production (IIP) rose 5.2% in February, a slight increase from January. Manufacturing grew by 6%, led by capital and infrastructure goods. However, consumer non-durables remained in contraction. Experts warned that future industrial activity might face pressure from geopolitical tensions and elevated energy costs despite the recent pickup.

articles.headline: War Robbing Street Mcap Drops Further ₹9.41L cr
articles.summary: Indian stock markets crashed over 2% on Monday as tensions in West Asia and surging crude oil prices triggered a massive sell-off. The BSE Sensex plummeted 1,635.67 points to close at 71,947.55, while the Nifty 50 dropped 488.20 points to 22,331.40. Investors lost approximately ₹9.41 lakh crore in market capitalization, marking the worst monthly performance since the Covid-19 crash in March 2020.

articles.headline: Sinking 11% in FY26 Sea of Troubles, Re at 94.83
articles.summary: The Indian rupee fell to an all-time low of 94.83 against the US dollar on Monday, marking a nearly 11% decline in the current fiscal year. The drop is driven by the escalating conflict in West Asia and surging crude oil prices. Despite intermittent interventions by the Reserve Bank of India to provide liquidity, the currency remained under intense pressure at the close of the trading year.

articles.headline: CII Lists Steps to Ease Pain of War-hit Cos
articles.summary: The Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) has requested the government to implement a temporary moratorium on loan repayments and a restructuring window for firms affected by the West Asia conflict. Proposals include fast-tracking GST refunds and expanding the credit guarantee scheme to support MSMEs facing liquidity crunches due to rising energy costs and supply chain disruptions.

articles.headline: Fiscal Deficit Until Feb Eases to 80.4% of Target
articles.summary: The central government's fiscal deficit for the first 11 months of the current financial year stood at 80.4% of the annual target, down from 85.8% a year ago. In absolute terms, the deficit reached ₹12.53 lakh crore against the target of ₹15.58 lakh crore. Higher net tax revenue collections and moderate expenditure growth contributed to the improved fiscal health.

articles.headline: Govt Cracks Down on Fertiliser Black Marketing
articles.summary: The Union government has initiated a crackdown on the illegal diversion and black marketing of fertilizers following reports of shortages. Authorities have warned of criminal proceedings and the cancellation of registration for dealers found hoarding stocks. A dedicated task group and a 24/7 war room have been established to monitor supply and ensure adequate availability for the kharif season.

articles.headline: Expect More Funky Moves from RBI on Rupee
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India may implement unconventional measures to manage the rupee's decline as it approaches the psychological 95-mark. Potential actions include special schemes to attract dollar inflows from NRIs or direct interventions in the offshore market. Analysts suggest the central bank is drawing from its emergency playbook to stabilize the currency amid high global energy prices.

articles.headline: Yield Tops 7% as Banks Stare at MTM Losses
articles.summary: The 10-year Indian government bond yield rose to 7.03% on Monday, the highest in over a year, causing concern for banks regarding mark-to-market (MTM) losses. The sharp rise is attributed to the rupee's volatility and expectations of a prolonged high-interest rate environment. Banks with large bond portfolios could see significant hits to their quarterly profits due to these valuation changes.

articles.headline: Pay More for F&O Trade from April 1
articles.summary: Trading in equity derivatives is set to become more expensive from April 1 as higher Securities Transaction Tax (STT) and exchange fees kick in. The government and the central bank aim to curb excessive retail speculation in the F&O segment. Market experts anticipate a 15-20% drop in trading volumes as higher costs impact the profitability of frequent and small-ticket traders.

articles.headline: Panic buying sparks sharp spike in fertilizer sales across India
articles.summary: Farmers are stockpiling fertilizers as government data for March shows a sharp spike in demand. By March 23, sales of urea reached 20.21 lakh tonnes, surpassing the monthly estimate of 14.96 lt. Significant increases were also seen in DAP and complex fertilizers. Higher demand was reported in Telangana, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, and Maharashtra.

articles.headline: Gram panchayats process over ₹3 lakh crore in online payments
articles.summary: More than 2.5 lakh gram panchayats have processed over ₹3 lakh crore via the eGramSwaraj digital platform since April 2019. In FY26 alone, ₹53,342 crore was transferred to vendors in real time. The system aims to curb mismanagement of rural funds by ensuring direct payments with transparent digital audit trails.

articles.headline: Government opens emergency window for PDS kerosene sales
articles.summary: The Centre notified a 60-day window allowing select petrol pumps to sell PDS superior kerosene oil for cooking and lighting. The move aims to assist migrant laborers and households facing shortages of commercial LPG imports due to the West Asia conflict. Seventeen States and UTs have already issued allocation orders for the commodity.

articles.headline: Cotton acreage likely to rise 20% on domestic price rebound
articles.summary: Cotton sowing in India's North Zone is expected to increase by up to 20% in the 2026 Kharif season. Sowing is buoyed by improved domestic prices and a predicted El Nino, which makes drought-tolerant cotton a safer bet for farmers than water-intensive crops like paddy. Sowing area is expected to reach at least 110 lakh hectares.

articles.headline: Australia-EU FTA raises concerns over Indian Basmati rice GI tag
articles.summary: The recent free trade agreement between Australia and the EU has sparked concerns that the EU may gain more protection for its spirit and wine GIs while stalling India’s recognition for Basmati rice. Experts urged India to insist on Basmati and Darjeeling tea protections in upcoming trade talks to prevent product genericisation.

articles.headline: Aluminium hits four-year peak following Iran strikes on West Asia
articles.summary: Aluminium prices surged on the London Metal Exchange as airstrikes in the Middle East raised risks of prolonged supply shocks. The metal traded at $3,424.5 a tonne, its highest since 2022. Closure of the Strait of Hormuz has restricted shipments to major export markets in the US and Europe.

articles.headline: Falling gold prices raise top-up call risks for gold lenders
articles.summary: A 15% drop in gold prices this month could trigger top-up calls by banks and NBFCs despite 25% margin buffers. Thomas Stephen of Anand Rathi Brokers noted that lenders may aggressively seek collateral top-ups for high loan-to-value ratios as prices hit ₹1.46 lakh per 10g. New tiered RBI LTV norms also take effect on Wednesday.

articles.headline: New policy to promote skill development in manufacturing sector
articles.summary: The government has announced a new policy aimed at enhancing skill development within the manufacturing sector. This aims to boost employment and industrial growth.

articles.headline: Government proposes incentives for electric vehicle adoption
articles.summary: The government is proposing new incentives to encourage the adoption of electric vehicles. This initiative aims to reduce pollution and promote sustainable transportation.

articles.headline: India industrial growth rises to 5.2 per cent in February
articles.summary: Industrial growth, measured by the Index of Industrial Production (IIP), rose to 5.2% in February from 5.1% in January. The manufacturing sector led the performance with 6% growth compared to 2.8% a year ago. Mining production slightly improved to 3.1%, while electricity generation grew by 2.3%. Analysts warn that the West Asia crisis may impact future production momentum.

articles.headline: Piped natural gas pipeline capacity growth slows in FY25
articles.summary: India's natural gas pipeline network stood at 239.472 million metric tonnes in FY25, representing a modest 2.23% growth compared to over 10% in 2022-23. Analysts suggest the industry is entering a normalization phase after a period of rapid build-out. Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Uttar Pradesh lead in capacity, though West Asia tensions have renewed the push for faster adoption.

articles.headline: Net claims of non-residents on India stand at $260.5 billion
articles.summary: According to RBI data, net claims of non-residents on India decreased to $260.5 billion at the end of December 2025. India’s international investment position improved, with the ratio of assets to liabilities rising to 82.1%. The shift was driven by higher growth in overseas financial assets held by Indian residents compared to foreign-owned assets within India.

articles.headline: Banks cannot record customer locker contents says Finance Minister Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman clarified in Parliament that banks are prohibited from recording or valuing contents stored in customer lockers. This statement follows queries regarding the safety and privacy of bank vaults. She emphasized that locker agreements are based on trust and banks must maintain differential insurance coverage based on current storage values.

articles.headline: Karnataka MSMEs drive state growth with six lakh enterprises in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Data from the Karnataka Economic Survey 2025-26 reveals that Bengaluru Urban district accounts for 6.3 lakh MSME units, generating over 51 lakh jobs. This growth has made Karnataka a leading industrial hub in India. However, the report also highlighted concerns regarding rising power tariffs and high land costs, which could potentially slow down small-scale industrial expansion.

articles.headline: India's energy imports from Africa rise amid West Asia volatility
articles.summary: India's procurement of crude oil and LNG from African nations has seen a significant increase as the country seeks to diversify its energy sources. Following disruptions in West Asia, the Petroleum Ministry reported that imports from Nigeria, Angola, and Algeria now account for nearly 20% of total energy supplies. This strategic shift aims to insulate the Indian economy from price shocks.

articles.headline: Rupee breaches 95 mark against dollar for the first time
articles.summary: The Indian Rupee hit a historic low, breaching the 95-mark against the US Dollar during intraday trading. Market analysts attribute the sharp decline to the escalating US-Iran conflict and rising global crude oil prices. The Reserve Bank of India reportedly intervened in the forex market to curb excessive volatility, but the currency ended the day at its lowest close in a decade.

articles.headline: West Asia war poses risk to India’s subsea internet cables
articles.summary: The conflict in West Asia has raised concerns regarding the safety of 17 submarine cables in the Red Sea that carry 60% of India's internet traffic. While a total blackout is unlikely due to rerouting capabilities, damage could lead to significant buffering and disrupted operations for global capability centres. Major operators like Airtel and Tata Communications remain at high risk.

articles.headline: Microsoft debuts Copilot upgrades allowing simultaneous use of multiple AI models
articles.summary: Microsoft has introduced new features to its Copilot research assistant, allowing users to utilize OpenAI’s GPT and Anthropic’s Claude models simultaneously. A new 'Critique' tool allows one model to review another's output for accuracy. The 'model Council' feature also enables side-by-side response comparisons, marking a shift toward bi-directional AI workflows for better quality control.

articles.headline: Government proposes binding social media oversight through IT rule amendments
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and IT has proposed draft changes to the IT Rules, 2021, making government advisories and orders legally binding for social media platforms. Failure to comply could lead to intermediaries losing 'safe harbour' legal immunity. The move would significantly expand state oversight on platforms like X, YouTube, and Facebook. Stakeholders have until April 14 to comment.

articles.headline: Indian industry seeks 'UPI moment' for mass-scale AI adoption
articles.summary: Experts at the Mint India Investment Summit stated that India’s AI breakthrough depends on government-to-consumer use cases reaching the scale of the Unified Payments Interface (UPI). While the IndiaAI Mission aims to acquire 18,000 GPUs to build high-performance compute capacity, challenges remain in data disparate systems and the need for human oversight to ensure responsible outcomes.

articles.headline: OpenAI cancels Sora video tool development to prioritize computing resources
articles.summary: OpenAI has abruptly shut down its Sora video-generation tool project to redirect limited AI chips toward more profitable agentic AI and coding products. The move shocked partner Disney, which had planned to invest $1 billion and use the tech for Disney+. OpenAI realized Sora's high energy costs and lack of profitability made it a liability ahead of a looming IPO.

articles.headline: West Asia war threatens Gulf funding for American AI industry infrastructure
articles.summary: The Iran war has put $2 trillion in projected Middle Eastern AI investments at risk while surging energy costs threaten US data centers. While software-focused labs like OpenAI and Anthropic are insulated by enterprise contracts, hardware players like Nvidia and hyperscalers are vulnerable. Natural gas, a major power source for data centers, has seen prices spike due to the regional conflict.

articles.headline: Indian IT services firms adapt billing models as GenAI disrupts industry
articles.summary: The adoption of Generative AI is forcing Indian IT firms like Infosys and Wipro to move toward outcome-based pricing as productivity gains reduce the need for large headcounts. A survey suggests GenAI could boost coding productivity by 45%. This shift threatens traditional manpower-based billing models, leading firms to experiment with new ways to capture value from AI-driven automation.

articles.headline: Popular Chinese AI chatbot DeepSeek suffers major seven hour outage
articles.summary: China’s popular DeepSeek AI chatbot experienced a seven-hour major outage, its longest since launching flagship models R1 and V3. The service disruption affected both web and app users. The outage comes amid the viral rise of the AI tool, which has gained significant traction globally over the past year. Developers cited a major system failure but did not elaborate on the cause.

articles.headline: Cyber Pirates at Large, India Readies Moats
articles.summary: CERT-In and financial regulators have issued high-level alerts to banks, energy firms, and telecom utilities following a surge in state-backed cyber espionage. Experts warn that groups from Europe, Russia, Israel, and Iran are targeting critical infrastructure. Utilities have begun stress tests to identify vulnerabilities like phishing as geopolitical tensions spill into digital domains.

articles.headline: MeitY Clears 29 GCMS Proposals Worth ₹7,104 cr
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and IT has approved 29 proposals worth ₹7,104 crore under the Government Chip Manufacturing Scheme (GCMS). The projects focus on semiconductor design, R&D, and manufacturing components. IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw stated that the move will help weed out non-serious players and strengthen India's domestic electronics ecosystem and supply chain resilience.

articles.headline: Proposed IT rules amendments seek scrutiny of user news posts
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and IT has proposed amendments to the IT Rules, 2021, to include user-generated news content under government oversight. The changes would bring influencers and independent creators hosting news-related content under the regulatory framework, requiring platforms to implementation binding diligeance and removal mechanisms.

articles.headline: Tech firm launches AI-powered tool for medical diagnostics
articles.summary: A technology firm has launched an AI-powered tool designed to assist in medical diagnostics. This innovation aims to improve accuracy and speed of disease detection.

articles.headline: Cybersecurity experts warn of new phishing campaign
articles.summary: Cybersecurity experts have issued a warning about a sophisticated new phishing campaign targeting users. They advise caution and vigilance against suspicious communications.

articles.headline: Researchers develop faster charging battery technology
articles.summary: Scientists have developed a new battery technology that allows for significantly faster charging. This breakthrough could revolutionize portable electronics and electric vehicles.

articles.headline: International conference on artificial intelligence concludes
articles.summary: An international conference on artificial intelligence concluded, bringing together experts to discuss advancements and ethical considerations in AI.

articles.headline: EV transition must be inclusive and sustainable, says Minister
articles.summary: Union Minister HD Kumaraswamy emphasized that India's electric mobility transition must focus on both urban and rural markets, specifically highlighting the importance of light electric vehicles (LEVs). He launched the industry-led platform LEAF to promote interoperability among infrastructure providers and technology partners like Ather Energy and Hero MotoCorp.

articles.headline: DeepSeek AI chatbot suffers longest global outage after viral surge
articles.summary: The popular AI chatbot DeepSeek experienced its longest outage to date, lasting over seven hours on Monday. The company attributed the failure to a massive surge in global traffic that overwhelmed its servers. Users across several continents reported the tool was completely unresponsive. DeepSeek has since restored services but warned of intermittent stability during peak hours.

articles.headline: Indian film content volumes grow as OTT releases decline in 2025
articles.summary: A Ficci-EY report reveals that Indian film content volumes grew by 5.1% in 2025, while OTT content declined by 6.9%. The year saw 14 Hindi films and five English films crossing the ₹100-crore box office mark. Television content (excluding news) also saw a contraction of 3.1%, reflecting a shift in consumer preferences toward theatrical releases over streaming formats.

articles.headline: Copyright clocks run out for iconic Bollywood songs from 1950s-60s
articles.summary: Iconic Hindi film songs are entering the public domain as their 60-year statutory copyright terms expire. This transition allows businesses like hotels and broadcasters to use recordings without licensing fees. While beneficial for users, it reduces income for legacy music labels. Experts note limited awareness among businesses that continue paying for music already in the public domain.

articles.headline: Government launches database to study unexplored ancient Indian manuscripts
articles.summary: The Union government informed Parliament about new schemes to study unexplored ancient manuscripts. The initiative includes onboarding institutions and states to launch a comprehensive database. The project aims to catalog and analyze historical texts that have remained largely unexplored to preserve India's cultural and academic heritage.

articles.headline: Indian diaspora in US celebrates cultural festival
articles.summary: The Indian diaspora in the United States celebrated a vibrant cultural festival, showcasing diverse traditions and fostering community bonds. The event highlighted Indian heritage.

articles.headline: Archaeological discovery sheds light on ancient civilization
articles.summary: An archaeological excavation has unearthed significant artifacts, providing new insights into an ancient civilization that once thrived in the region. The findings are of historical importance.

articles.headline: Historical monument receives UNESCO World Heritage status
articles.summary: A significant historical monument has been granted UNESCO World Heritage status, recognizing its outstanding universal value and cultural importance. This will boost tourism and conservation efforts.

articles.headline: Local artist showcases traditional craft at international exhibition
articles.summary: A local artist is showcasing traditional Indian craft at an international exhibition. The artist's work highlights intricate craftsmanship and cultural heritage.

articles.headline: Indian film festival to be held in major global cities
articles.summary: An international Indian film festival is scheduled to be held in several major global cities. The event will showcase contemporary Indian cinema and its diversity.

articles.headline: Def Leppard guitarist Phil Collen buys artwork by Bengaluru-based artist
articles.summary: Phil Collen, lead guitarist of the British rock band Def Leppard, has acquired an artwork titled 'Human Nature' by Bengaluru artist Devika Sundar. The piece, created using watercolors and pen, explores the inner workings of the human body. Collen discovered the work during a private exhibition in Bengaluru while the band was in the city for a performance.

articles.headline: Italian museum reports theft of Renoir and Matisse masterpieces
articles.summary: Three priceless paintings by masters Pierre-Auguste Renoir, Paul Cezanne, and Henri Matisse were stolen from a museum in Italy. Thieves reportedly bypassed the security system during a late-night heist and escaped with works estimated to be worth over $10 million. Italian police have launched a nationwide manhunt and alerted international art databases to prevent the sale of the works.

articles.headline: Government orders electronics firms to develop in-house product design capabilities
articles.summary: Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw has given electronics manufacturers 15 days to submit plans for in-house product design and six-sigma engineering certification. The move aims to move India beyond simple assembly into deep-tech manufacturing. Failure to comply could result in withheld incentives. The ministry recently approved 29 new component projects with cumulative investments of ₹7,104 crore.

articles.headline: Retail brokers face revenue hit as West Asia war dampens sentiment
articles.summary: India’s retail brokers are bracing for a 20-30% revenue decline as the West Asia conflict discourages trading. The active retail client base has already shrunk 8% to 44.81 million in FY26. Major brokers like Groww, Zerodha, and Angel One have seen active user drops. High oil prices and a weakening rupee are expected to further intensify the 'leaky bucket' dynamic of client attrition.

articles.headline: Agritech startup Cropcoin raises ₹12 crore for Bihar and Jharkhand expansion
articles.summary: Cropcoin Technologies (Pehle Jaisa) raised ₹12 crore in a pre-series A round led by Unicorn India Ventures. The startup plans to expand its decentralized supply chain model from 10 districts to all 66 districts in Bihar and Jharkhand. The company also intends to strengthen its presence in the North East region over the next three years to improve agricultural logistics.

articles.headline: Startups leverage trust and local language to capture hinterland consumers
articles.summary: Startups like Wealthy, Traya, and Frnd are finding success in India's Tier 2+ markets by focusing on local nuance and trust-based relationships. Moving away from 'Big City' data models, these firms use local creators and voice-first interfaces to build habits. Digital penetration and UPI familiarity are cited as major drivers for growth in non-urban industrial towns.

articles.headline: Kalpataru Projects warns of revenue hit from West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Kalpataru Projects International expects a potential revenue impact of ₹200-300 crore in Q4FY26 due to supply challenges in West Asia. The region accounts for 10-11% of the firm's ₹63,300-crore order book. Despite delays, the management remains optimistic about 20% revenue growth in FY27, supported by robust domestic demand for power transmission and water infrastructure projects.

articles.headline: Private equity moves toward buyouts and continuation fund structures
articles.summary: India's private equity sector is seeing a rise in buyout opportunities as mature assets look to professionalize. Firms like Bain Capital and TPG are focusing on bolt-on acquisitions to accelerate value creation. New structures, such as single-asset continuation vehicles, are emerging to help firms stay invested in high-performing assets like the NSE beyond typical fund cycles.

articles.headline: India's M&A landscape shows resilience despite global geopolitical volatility
articles.summary: Top investment bankers report that Indian M&A momentum remains strong as investors take 5-10 year views, pricing in short-term risks like the West Asia conflict. Cross-border deals, including the $3 billion RBL Bank stake purchase by Emirates NBD, highlight the maturity of the ecosystem. Private equity and venture capital exits reached approximately $120 billion between 2021 and 2025.

articles.headline: Electronics firms monitor war impact as component prices and exports fluctuate
articles.summary: Indian electronics manufacturing services (EMS) firms are in 'wait-and-watch' mode as the West Asia war disrupts tech supply chains. While firms like Dixon and Syrma have pass-through clauses for rising costs, a potential $3 billion mobile phone export loss looms. Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw stated that localization is ongoing and the industry expects the conflict to be short-term.

articles.headline: Global Hotel Alliance CEO sees India as top long-term travel prize
articles.summary: GHA CEO Christopher Hartley stated that while the West Asia conflict impacts short-term Gulf travel, India's outbound market remains a massive long-term opportunity. Indian outbound spending is projected to grow to $44.8 billion by 2032. Despite airspace disruptions and rerouting, leisure travel remains resilient, with India expected to become a top source of global travelers within five years.

articles.headline: Inox Air Products plans $1 billion Mumbai IPO with global bankers
articles.summary: Industrial and medical gas manufacturer Inox Air Products is planning a $1 billion initial public offering in Mumbai. The company has appointed Kotak, JPMorgan, and Citi to manage the share sale. The firm is a joint venture between the Inox Group and American company Air Products and Chemicals. It plans to file its draft prospectus within a month, aiming to tap into India's IPO market.

articles.headline: Former HDFC Bank chairman Atanu Chakraborty cites ethics for shock resignation
articles.summary: Atanu Chakraborty, the former chairman of HDFC Bank, revealed that his resignation was tied to ethical concerns over how the bank managed regulatory lapses in Dubai. Chakraborty criticized the bank's characterization of the issues as 'technical lapses,' stating they were not rooted in values. The Dubai regulator had restricted the bank from adding new clients last September following compliance failures.

articles.headline: JioStar terminates IPL broadcast deal in Bangladesh following payment defaults
articles.summary: Mukesh Ambani’s JioStar has terminated its Indian Premier League (IPL) broadcast agreement in Bangladesh, citing payment defaults by its local partner. The move follows a ban on IPL broadcasts in Bangladesh triggered by diplomatic tensions. The termination means Bangladeshi viewers will lack a local broadcaster for the current season even if the government eventually lifts the ban.

articles.headline: Vale targets India for iron ore growth as China’s demand fades
articles.summary: Top iron ore miner Vale SA is ramping up shipments to India, aiming to capitalize on one of the world's fastest-growing steel markets as China's output plateaus. Vale projects India's steel capacity to triple to 500 million tonnes by 2050. The miner also seeks to source, blend, and trade ore globally from India, marking a strategic shift toward South Asia for long-term growth.

articles.headline: Financial sector experts call for tighter rules against product mis-selling
articles.summary: New draft regulations from the RBI targeting financial product mis-selling have been welcomed but criticized for major gaps. Experts argue that the rules must cover agents and brokers, not just bank employees, and specify documentation standards for suitability analysis. Currently, many mis-selling complaints in life insurance occur in channels outside the RBI's jurisdictional scope.

articles.headline: Indian stock markets end FY26 with significant losses amid global tensions
articles.summary: Indian markets ended FY26 on a weak note, with the Sensex falling 2.22% and Nifty dropping 2.14% on the final trading day. For the full fiscal year, the Nifty 50 fell 5% and Sensex declined 7%. The downturn was driven by the Iran-US war, a weakening rupee, and record foreign investor outflows, which reached a 34-year high in equities.

articles.headline: Foreign institutional investors pull record 1.8 lakh crore from Indian equities
articles.summary: Foreign portfolio investors withdrew a record ₹1.8 lakh crore from Indian equities in FY26, the highest outflow in 34 years. The exodus was driven by slowing earnings growth, a weakening rupee, and higher global interest rates. Market analysts noted that outflows intensified in March due to the West Asia war, making FY26 a forgettable year for the Indian markets.

articles.headline: Exports of Diesel in (Re)Fine Form
articles.summary: Indian refined oil exports surged 20% month-on-month in March as domestic refiners prioritized international markets to capture higher margins. Exports reached 12.90 million barrels, up from 10.74 million in February. The increase comes despite global geopolitical volatility, with mid-distillate products like diesel leading the growth as refiners adjust production to meet global demand.

articles.headline: Everything, Everywhere, All in 10 Minutes: Quick Commerce Booms
articles.summary: India's quick commerce startups are rapidly expanding beyond groceries into electronics, fashion, and beauty products. Major players like Blinkit, Zepto, and Instamart aim to scale significantly by March 2026. Despite a funding slowdown in broader tech, the segment raised over $586 million between January 2025 and March 2026, driven by high consumer demand for 10-minute deliveries.

articles.headline: Airtel, Tata Telcos Pay First Instalment of AGR Dues
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel and Tata Teleservices have paid the first instalment of their adjusted gross revenue (AGR) liabilities to the Department of Telecommunications. Airtel paid approximately ₹9,200 crore, while Tata Teleservices contributed about ₹4,100 crore. The payments follow the Supreme Court's refusal to grant further relief on the total ₹1.47 lakh crore industry-wide debt.

articles.headline: Novo Nordisk to Slash Ozempic Price Amid Deluge
articles.summary: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk plans to reduce the price of its popular GLP-1 weight loss and diabetes drug, Ozempic, by 30-50% in India. The move follows the entry of low-cost generic versions from Indian firms like Sun Pharma and Zydus Lifesciences. The company aims to protect its market share as domestic competitors launch affordable alternatives priced around ₹4,000 per month.

articles.headline: Airtel’s Nxtra Raises $1b, Valued at $3.1b
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel's data center arm, Nxtra Data, has raised $1 billion from a consortium of private equity firms including Carlyle, GIP, and Alpha Wave Global. The funding round values the company at $3.1 billion. Nxtra intends to use the capital to expand its data center footprint across India, aiming to build a 1 GW capacity to support the country's growing digital and AI infrastructure.

articles.headline: Ashwini Vaishnaw warns electronics firms over manufacturing targets
articles.summary: Electronics Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw warned manufacturers that fund disbursements under the ECMS could be halted if targets for structured design programs and Six Sigma standards are not met. He gave firms a 15-day deadline to update the government on progress toward product design and local sourcing goals.

articles.headline: Wipro bets on regional flavours and convenience in food business
articles.summary: Wipro Foods is scaling regional brands Nirapara and Brahmins while expanding into ethnic breakfast and ready-to-cook categories. President Anil Chugh stated the company is targeting state-specific taste palettes rather than national standardization, with brands like Brahmins consolidating leadership in the Kerala market.

articles.headline: India's exports grow 3.14% in April to $34.92 billion
articles.summary: India's merchandise exports rose by 3.14% to $34.92 billion in April 2024. However, the trade deficit widened to $19.11 billion due to a 10.2% increase in imports.

articles.headline: Sensex, Nifty hit record highs on strong corporate earnings, global cues
articles.summary: Indian stock markets, the Sensex and Nifty, touched new all-time highs driven by robust corporate earnings and positive global sentiment. Investors are optimistic about economic growth.

articles.headline: New tech startup raises $10 million in Series A funding
articles.summary: A new Indian technology startup has successfully raised $10 million in its Series A funding round. The investment will fuel product development and market expansion.

articles.headline: Start-up develops sustainable packaging alternative
articles.summary: An Indian start-up has developed a biodegradable and sustainable packaging alternative. This innovation addresses environmental concerns related to plastic waste.

articles.headline: International trade fair showcases Indian products
articles.summary: An international trade fair is showcasing a wide range of Indian products and services. The event aims to boost export opportunities and promote global trade.

articles.headline: Medical device makers face working capital distress due to war
articles.summary: The Association of Indian Medical Device Industry has called for fast-tracking GST refunds and import duty rebates as war-driven input costs rise. Manufacturers report a 50% price increase in medical-grade polymers and a 20% rise in packaging costs. While no current shortages exist, companies have already hiked ex-factory prices by up to 20% to manage expenses.

articles.headline: Rentomojo IPO faces legal challenge from former COO
articles.summary: Furniture rental platform Rentomojo’s IPO plans are facing a hurdle at the NCLT. Former COO Ajay Nain has filed a petition alleging oppression and mismanagement, seeking to halt the public issue over a 2023 share transaction dispute. The company has filed caveats in response. Rentomojo recently filed draft papers for a primary raise of ₹150 crore alongside an offer for sale.

articles.headline: ARCs expect improved asset management growth in FY27
articles.summary: Asset Reconstruction Companies (ARCs) anticipate better growth in the next financial year as the lender asset-quality cycle turns. While bank NPAs are currently at multi-decadal lows, the impact of the West Asia war on MSMEs and retail loans is expected to create new opportunities for ARCs to acquire and manage stressed assets as systemic pressure builds.

articles.headline: Wipro Foods focuses on regional depth and convenience products
articles.summary: Wipro Foods is doubling down on regional brands like Brahmins and Nirapara, targeting high-growth categories like ready-to-cook meals. The company has launched the 'Brahmins Xpress Mix' range to meet consumer demand for home-style food with minimal effort. The strategy prioritizes deepening presence in specific states over pan-India standardization to match local taste palettes.

articles.headline: Gold gains on safe-haven demand amid West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Gold prices rose for a second straight session as investors sought safe-haven assets due to rising inflation concerns from the West Asia conflict. Spot gold increased 1.6% to $4,564 per ounce, while US gold futures for April delivery rose to $4,594. Despite recent gains, the metal is headed for a monthly decline as volatility continues to impact commodity markets.

articles.headline: Malaysian palm oil futures hit highest level since late 2024
articles.summary: Palm oil futures reached a 15-month high on expectations that Indonesia will continue with its palm-oil-based biodiesel program. The June delivery contract rose to 4,772 ringgit ($1,185.30). The price surge is supported by improving global demand and expectations of a deficit monsoon due to potential El Nino conditions, which could impact future production levels.

articles.headline: AWL Agri to scale Tops brand revenue to ₹1,000 crore
articles.summary: AWL Agri Business aims to double the revenue of its acquired Tops brand to ₹1,000 crore in the next few years. The brand, known for ketchups and pickles, currently generates ₹500 crore. The expansion strategy involves strengthening presence in Northern India before widening distribution to other geographies by leveraging manufacturing and operational strengths.

articles.headline: Tube Investments infuses ₹250 crore in electric mobility arm
articles.summary: Tube Investments of India (TIL) will invest approximately ₹250 crore into its subsidiary, TI Clean Mobility Pvt Ltd, through convertible preference shares. The funds will be used to scale up operations in the electric vehicle segment. TICMPL operates across various electric mobility platforms and intends to use the capital for subsidiary expansion.

articles.headline: Quick commerce platforms pivot to assortment to drive growth
articles.summary: Indian quick commerce players like Zepto and Blinkit are shifting focus from rapid dark store expansion to increasing product variety. Zepto now offers nearly 40,000 SKUs as platforms target higher average order values through non-grocery items like electronics and toys. The shift aims to improve customer retention and capitalize on demand for general merchandise.

articles.headline: Falling gold prices increase margin call risks for lenders
articles.summary: A 15% drop in gold prices this month due to the US-Iran conflict could trigger top-up calls for gold loan customers. While NBFCs maintain margin buffers, the decline toward ₹1.46 lakh per 10g is nearing threshold levels. Lenders are closely monitoring loan-to-value ratios as the RBI's new tiered framework for gold-backed credit access comes into effect.

articles.headline: Former HDFC Chairman Atanu Chakraborty clarifies board dispute over bond action
articles.summary: Atanu Chakraborty, former HDFC Chairman, clarified his resignation was due to differences with the board regarding bond actions. In an interview, he stated that the incentive structures were not aligned with long-term governance and stakeholder interests. He emphasized that the board should ensure internal practices remain transparent to protect minority shareholders.

articles.headline: Mistral AI raises 830 million dollars for European data centers
articles.summary: French startup Mistral AI has raised $830 million in its latest funding round to build and operate sovereign AI data centers in Europe. The move is designed to reduce the continent's reliance on American tech giants like Amazon and Microsoft. The new facilities will focus on privacy and high-performance computing for government and corporate clients across the European Union.

articles.headline: Slow dispute resolution costs India estimated 2% of GDP annually
articles.summary: Legal experts at the Mint Investment Summit highlighted that India's inefficient dispute resolution system deters investment and erodes capital. Prolonged litigation and weak contract enforcement are estimated to cost 1.5-2% of national GDP. Panellists called for more institutional arbitration and domain expertise to resolve complex commercial disputes faster than the current court system.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court allows Dr Reddy’s limited supply of semaglutide drug
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has permitted Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories to supply remaining stocks of its semaglutide drug Olymviq to government hospitals after a 30-day market clearance window. The firm agreed to transition to the brand name Olymra following a trademark suit by Novo Nordisk. Semaglutide is a critical treatment for type-2 diabetes and weight loss, and went off-patent in India on March 20.

articles.headline: Tax experts advocate for structural reforms to reduce adversarial taxation
articles.summary: Calls for a non-adversarial tax system require separating investigative and adjudicatory functions to reduce institutional bias. While only 1% of returns face scrutiny, the lack of time-bound correction for errors imposes severe burdens on businesses. Experts suggest that a credible first-instance appellate stage is essential to improve trust and voluntary compliance among taxpayers.

articles.headline: ED challenges Arvind Kejriwal's acquittal in summons case at High Court
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate moved the Delhi High Court against a trial court's acquittal of former Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal. The case involves allegations that Kejriwal skipped multiple ED summonses during the investigation into the Delhi excise policy. The High Court is scheduled to take up the matter on Wednesday.

articles.headline: President Murmu grants assent to Transgender Persons Amendment and Finance Bills
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu has granted assent to the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026. The new law redefines the category of transgender and removes provisions for self-perceived gender identity. The President also gave assent to the Finance Bill, 2026 and the Appropriation (No. 2) Bill, 2026, officially enacting the proposed laws.

articles.headline: Three Bengaluru traffic police suspended for extorting money from motorists
articles.summary: Three Bengaluru traffic police personnel, including an ASI and two constables, were suspended for extorting money from motorists. An internal inquiry found they deliberately switched off their mandatory body-worn cameras to avoid scrutiny while collecting illegal payments in the High Grounds area. The department reiterated that such misuse of authority will face serious consequences.

articles.headline: Senior commercial tax official arrested for accepting six lakh rupee bribe
articles.summary: The Karnataka Lokayukta arrested Deputy Commissioner Bharat Hegde for allegedly accepting a ₹6 lakh bribe to settle a GST matter. The official was caught red-handed in Hubballi following a complaint from a petrol bunk owner. The complainant alleged the officer demanded the payment to resolve a notice regarding Input Tax Credit claims totaling ₹60 lakh.

articles.headline: Ten arrested in Hyderabad for selling commercial LPG at exorbitant rates
articles.summary: Hyderabad Police arrested ten individuals for illegally selling commercial LPG cylinders at highly inflated prices to needy customers. Acting on credible information, a task force seized 414 cylinders from the suspects. The arrests come amid a broader shortage of commercial gas linked to global supply disruptions.

articles.headline: Telangana Hate Speech Bill referred to select committee following objections
articles.summary: The Telangana Legislative Assembly referred a bill aimed at curbing hate speech and hate crimes to a select committee. The move follows objections from BJP, AIMIM, and CPI members regarding vague definitions and potential misuse to suppress dissent. The proposed law includes punishments of up to 10 years in prison but faces concerns over holding entire organizations liable.

articles.headline: Supreme Court rules state must pay compensation for poll duty accidents
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that state governments are liable for compensation when vehicles requisitioned for election duty are involved in accidents. The court held that liability rests with the requisitioning authority rather than private owners or insurers because control shifts to the state. This settles a legal battle stemming from a 2010 fatal bus accident in Madhya Pradesh.

articles.headline: ED questions TMC candidate Debasish Kumar over Kolkata land deals
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate questioned Trinamool Congress candidate Debasish Kumar regarding alleged land deals in South Kolkata. Kumar, who won the high-profile Rashbehari seat in 2021, is seeking re-election. The questioning took place in connection with sensitive financial transactions as part of an ongoing federal investigation into land-related irregularities.

articles.headline: Five held for gangrape of eleven-year-old girl in Ujjain
articles.summary: Madhya Pradesh police arrested three men and detained two minors for the gangrape of an 11-year-old girl in Ujjain. The accused reportedly filmed the assault and threatened the victim. They face charges under the POCSO Act, SC/ST Act, and IT Act. The minors have been sent to a correctional home, while the adults were remanded to jail after the victim revealed her ordeal to her family.

articles.headline: Delhi court orders deeper probe into foreign nationals using drones
articles.summary: A Delhi court extended the custody of seven foreign nationals, including six Ukrainians, investigated for illegal entry and drone use. The NIA is probing potential links between the accused and rebel ethnic groups in India and Myanmar. The court questioned the objectives of their travel and whether they were providing drone training to insurgents.

articles.headline: Inmate booked for harassing POCSO victim via video calls from prison
articles.summary: A man lodged in a Dharwad jail for a POCSO case was booked for allegedly harassing the victim through video calls from inside the prison. The case was registered on March 14 after the victim reported the harassment. The incident has raised serious questions regarding the illegal use of mobile phones and internet access by inmates in correctional facilities.

articles.headline: Supreme Court criticizes Gujarat High Court for loss of original records
articles.summary: The Supreme Court slammed the Gujarat High Court administration for the disappearance of original documents from a civil suit record. The court ordered an FIR and a criminal probe, questioning how accountability was not fixed earlier. The missing property receipts were central to the defense's case, and the SC set aside a lower court order that had closed the issue.

articles.headline: Israel's Parliament passes law mandating death penalty for certain offences
articles.summary: The Israeli Knesset passed a law approving the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis. The controversial bill was supported by far-right parties seeking to escalate punishments for nationalistic crimes. This marks a significant shift in Israel's judicial policy, concluding a years-long push for capital punishment in specific security-related cases.

articles.headline: Vedanta Moves SC against JAL’s Resolution Plan
articles.summary: Anil Agarwal-owned Vedanta has approached the Supreme Court to challenge the ₹14,500-crore debt resolution plan for Jaypee Infratech (JAL). Vedanta alleges that the insolvency process lacked transparency and failed to maximize value for creditors. The company claims its own bid was superior and had been unfairly rejected by the committee of creditors during the proceedings.

articles.headline: Vedanta challenges NCLT approval of Adani’s bid for JAL
articles.summary: Mining giant Vedanta Ltd has moved the Supreme Court seeking a stay on the NCLT order approving Adani Group's ₹14,535 crore bid to acquire Jaiprakash Associates Ltd (JAL). The appeal follows the NCLAT's refusal to stay implementation of the plan. Adani Enterprises has filed a caveat to be heard before any order is passed.

articles.headline: Rentomojo’s IPO plans hit legal hurdle in NCLT dispute
articles.summary: Furniture rental platform Rentomojo faces a legal challenge after former co-founder Ajay Nain moved the NCLT alleging oppression and mismanagement. Nain is seeking to restrain the proposed IPO, which includes a ₹150-crore fresh issue and an offer for sale. The dispute pertains to a 2023 share transaction involving an employee benefit trust.

articles.headline: SC seeks govt response on plea for independent probe into Adani
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the government to respond to a petition seeking an independent probe into allegations against the Adani Group. The plea cites reports of stock manipulation.

articles.headline: Supreme Court hears arguments on electoral reforms plea
articles.summary: The Supreme Court heard arguments on a petition advocating for significant electoral reforms. The plea seeks measures to ensure greater transparency and fairness in elections.

articles.headline: Interfaith live-in relationships are not an offence says Allahabad High Court
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that interfaith live-in relationships are not a criminal offence under any law. Granting protection to a couple, the court stated it is the state's duty to protect individual choices and privacy. The ruling emphasized that personal liberty cannot be infringed upon by police interference as long as both parties are consenting adults.

articles.headline: Supreme Court refuses relief to KM Sharma over 2025 detention
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has denied interim relief to former MP KM Sharma, who was arrested following the 2025 Gen Z movement crackdown. The court has requested a detailed written explanation from authorities justifying the legal grounds for his continued detention. The case has raised concerns regarding civil liberties and the state's power during periods of civil unrest.

articles.headline: Three Bengaluru cops suspended for taking bribes and disabling body cameras
articles.summary: Three traffic police personnel in Bengaluru have been suspended for allegedly accepting bribes from motorists and deliberately turning off their body-worn cameras. The action follows an internal probe where the officers were caught on hidden cameras during a late-night check. The Bengaluru Police Commissioner reiterated that any tampering with surveillance equipment will lead to dismissal.

articles.headline: London properties of sanctioned Iranian banker Ali Ansari frozen by UK
articles.summary: British authorities have frozen high-end London real estate linked to Iranian banker Ali Ansari, worth approximately $120 million. Officials link the funds to Iran’s Revolutionary Guard. The properties, located on Billionaires’ Row, have fallen into disrepair following sanctions. The move is part of a broader crackdown on global financial networks used to fund the Iranian regime's military.

articles.headline: Trump threatens to destroy Iran energy assets if talks fail
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to 'completely obliterate' Iranian electric plants and oil wells if a deal is not reached and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. The warning comes as 3,500 US Marines arrive in West Asia. Brent crude oil prices have surged 2.5% to $115 per barrel, stoking global fears of slower economic growth and faster inflation due to energy supply disruptions.

articles.headline: Iran rejects US peace proposals as unrealistic amid regional escalation
articles.summary: Tehran has dismissed US proposals to end the month-long West Asia war as 'excessive and illogical.' Iran continues to demand war reparations and an end to hostilities on all fronts, including Lebanon. Tensions rose as Yemen's Houthis launched missiles at Israel, and the Israeli military carried out strikes on Tehran and Beirut. Iran's parliament is also reviewing its exit from the nuclear treaty.

articles.headline: WTO talks fail after Brazil blocks extension of e-commerce duty moratorium
articles.summary: World Trade Organization ministerial talks in Cameroon collapsed after Brazil blocked a plan to prolong the moratorium on electronic transmission duties. The failure to extend the ban on duties for digital downloads and streaming is a major setback for global trade. The WTO is facing increasing pressure as economic nationalism sidelines the body and the US retreats from multilateralism.

articles.headline: Russia increases military and drone support for Iran against US forces
articles.summary: Russia has emerged as a winner in the Iran war as surging oil prices boost its economy. Moscow is now providing satellite imagery and drone technology to help Iran target US forces in the region. The Kremlin is using its support for Iran as a bargaining chip with Washington, aiming to maintain its influence in the Middle East as its partnerships elsewhere are squeezed by US policies.

articles.headline: Donald Trump threatens destruction of Iran energy and water infrastructure
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump threatened the total destruction of Iran's energy resources and vital civilian infrastructure if a war-ending deal is not reached quickly. Trump suggested American troops could seize the Kharg Island oil hub. While Trump claims diplomatic progress, Tehran denies direct negotiations as the conflict continues to impact global oil and water resources.

articles.headline: Indian national killed in Iranian strike on Kuwaiti desalination plant
articles.summary: An Indian worker was killed and others were injured when an Iranian strike hit a power and water desalination plant in Kuwait. This brings the total number of Indian fatalities in the ongoing West Asia conflict to eight. India’s external affairs ministry expressed condolences and called for restraint and de-escalation through dialogue and diplomacy.

articles.headline: Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau to retire following language controversy
articles.summary: Air Canada announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire by the end of the third quarter. The decision follows intense criticism over an English-only condolence video regarding a deadly crash, which was seen as insensitive in officially bilingual Canada. Prime Minister Mark Carney stated that it is essential for the airline's next leader to be bilingual.

articles.headline: NATO defenses intercept Iranian ballistic missile over Turkish airspace
articles.summary: An Iranian ballistic missile was shot down by NATO air defenses after entering Turkish airspace, marking the fourth such incident during the current Middle East conflict. Turkey's Defense Ministry confirmed the interception, while Tehran denied involvement in previous launches. NATO stated it remains prepared to defend all allies as Iran continues to target regional US military interests.

articles.headline: French authorities investigate Iranian link to foiled Paris bomb plot
articles.summary: France is investigating a suspected Iranian link to a thwarted bomb attack outside a Bank of America office in Paris. Five suspects are in custody. Interior Minister Laurent Nunez noted the modus operandi matches other recent European attacks claimed by pro-Iranian groups. Intelligence services believe Iran may be using common criminals as proxies for targeted actions against US interests.

articles.headline: Spain closes its airspace to US aircraft involved in Iran conflict
articles.summary: Spain has officially closed its airspace to US military planes involved in the war with Iran. Defense Minister Margarita Robles stated the move follows the government's stance that US-Israeli actions in the Middle East are illegal and unjust. The decision complicates US logistics, forcing bombers and transport planes to use alternative, longer routes for regional operations.

articles.headline: Pakistan Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar injured in fall during summit
articles.summary: Pakistan’s Deputy PM and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar suffered a hairline fracture in his shoulder after slipping at a reception ceremony. The incident occurred during a summit involving foreign ministers from Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt. Dar's son confirmed the diagnosis following the fall, which took place while the minister was greeting international counterparts.

articles.headline: Russia expels British diplomat over alleged subversive intelligence activities
articles.summary: Russia's FSB expelled British diplomat Albertus Gerhardus Janse Van Rensburg, accusing him of working as a spy. Moscow claimed the embassy secretary engaged in subversive activities that threatened Russian security. The move marks a further deterioration in UK-Russia relations, with the diplomat ordered to leave the country immediately following the espionage allegations.

articles.headline: Senior General Min Aung Hlaing resigns as Myanmar's military chief
articles.summary: Min Aung Hlaing stepped down as Myanmar’s armed forces chief to seek the presidency in a military-backed civilian administration. He handed command to Ye Win Oo five years after leading the 2021 coup. The move follows widely criticized elections and comes amid a raging civil war that has displaced millions and caused massive fatalities across the country.

articles.headline: Heavy fire exchanged between Pakistan and Afghanistan along the border
articles.summary: Pakistan and Afghanistan traded heavy artillery fire in the Kunar and Bajur border regions, ending a brief pause in hostilities. The clashes resulted in civilian casualties, including women and children. The escalation comes even as Islamabad expressed interest in hosting de-escalation talks between the US and Iran, complicating regional security dynamics.

articles.headline: Hundreds protest in Nepal seeking release of former PM KP Sharma Oli
articles.summary: Protesters in Kathmandu demanded the release of former PM KP Sharma Oli, who was arrested for his role in the 2025 Gen Z movement crackdown. Oli and ex-home minister Ramesh Lekhak were detained in pre-dawn raids. A court extended Oli's detention for five days while also investigating money laundering allegations against former energy minister Deepak Khadka.

articles.headline: Bruises and Scars Starting to Show on UAE Economy
articles.summary: The UAE economy is showing signs of stress from the regional conflict, with sectors like hospitality and retail seeing consumption drops of up to 30%. Remittance flows to India may be impacted as some companies implement pay cuts and unpaid leave. Business groups have reportedly called for government support and a temporary freeze on certain fees to manage the economic slowdown.

articles.headline: Ready for Peace Or Destruction: Trump to Iran
articles.summary: Former US President Donald Trump has issued a stark warning to Iran, stating it must agree to a peace deal or face the total destruction of its nuclear and military infrastructure. In a televised address, Trump claimed the US has identified key targets, including oil facilities and water plants. The threat has escalated regional tensions and sent global oil markets into a state of high alert.

articles.headline: WTO e-commerce moratorium lapses as India opposes extension
articles.summary: The global moratorium on electronic transmission duties has effectively ended as developing nations refused an extension at the WTO MC14. This allows countries to potentially impose tariffs on digital items like movies, software, and e-books. India and Brazil argued the moratorium resulted in significant revenue losses for developing economies.

articles.headline: India-US hold strategic dialogue on defence cooperation
articles.summary: India and the United States held a strategic dialogue to enhance defence cooperation. Discussions focused on joint exercises and technological collaboration.

articles.headline: South Korea weighs driving curbs to manage energy shock
articles.summary: South Korea may expand vehicle driving restrictions to the general public if oil prices breach $120 per barrel. The country has already imposed license-plate-based restrictions for civil servants at government buildings. This would be the first such move for the general public since the 1991 Gulf War, reflecting deep concerns over energy supply security due to the Iran war.

articles.headline: WTO moratorium on e-commerce taxes effectively lapses
articles.summary: A 28-year moratorium on e-commerce taxes has lapsed as developing nations, led by India, South Africa, and Brazil, opposed its extension at the WTO. The move allows countries to potentially impose customs duties on digital transmissions like software and streaming services. Critics argue the moratorium primarily benefited developed countries and major US tech firms.

articles.headline: Trump threatens to destroy Iran's oil islands if deal not reached
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has warned Iran of potential strikes on its oil islands and energy infrastructure unless negotiations proceed. Tensions have spiked as the US monitors Iranian missile movements while considering military action. The US has reportedly privately messaged Iran about 'fractures' in its leadership, while Trump remains defiant on seeking a new agreement.

articles.headline: Nepal health minister is an AIIMS-trained nurse in a first
articles.summary: Nepal's new Health Minister, Dr. Latha Venkatesan, is a former nursing professional trained at Delhi’s AIIMS. She is the first nurse to head the health ministry in Nepal's history. Venkatesan served as the principal of the College of Nursing at AIIMS for nearly 14 years. Her appointment is seen as a move to bring professional clinical expertise to the nation's healthcare policy.

articles.headline: US missile strikes school and sports hall in Iran during combat
articles.summary: An investigation into recent US-Iran hostilities revealed that a US-made ballistic missile hit a sports hall and a school in Iran. Visual evidence shows the facility, located near a military base, suffered significant damage. While the US claimed the target was an IRGC facility, Iranian officials reported that the strike caused casualties among civilians using the hall for local events.

articles.headline: Pakistan prepares to host US and Iran for peace talks
articles.summary: Pakistan has offered to host high-level peace negotiations between the United States and Iran following a week of direct military strikes. The Pakistani foreign ministry stated it has received positive signals from both nations. Islamabad has designated a secure venue for the talks, aiming to de-escalate regional tensions that have threatened global energy supplies and maritime trade.

articles.headline: Noida International Airport declared ready for commercial flight operations
articles.summary: Tata Projects announced that the Noida International Airport has secured all regulatory approvals and is ready for commercial operations. The construction phase, including the terminal building, runway, and airside infrastructure, is now complete. The airport is expected to significantly enhance connectivity in the National Capital Region and serve as a major hub for Northern India.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh CM Chandrababu Naidu inaugurates major welfare housing project
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N. Chandrababu Naidu inaugurated a TIDCO housing complex in Tirupati, marking the start of 2.5 lakh statewide housewarming ceremonies. The NDA coalition government has already distributed three lakh houses earlier this year. The project is a core part of the state's welfare agenda to provide permanent housing for eligible beneficiaries.

articles.headline: India says Won't Nationalise Oilfields to Attract Foreign Majors
articles.summary: The Indian government has assured global investors it will not nationalize oilfields or dilute international arbitration rights. This commitment comes as India launches the XIth offshore exploration licensing round offering 21 blocks. The move aims to fire up domestic output and reduce import dependency, which has been exacerbated by global supply disruptions and high crude prices.

articles.headline: Piped natural gas capacity growth slows to 2.23% in FY25
articles.summary: PNG capacity additions slowed sharply from 10.57% in FY23 to 2.23% in FY25. Data from the Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board indicates a cooling phase after years of rapid expansion. Gujarat leads in total capacity, followed by Maharashtra and Uttar Pradesh, as the industry transitions from execution to operational phases.

articles.headline: EV transition must be inclusive and sustainable, says Minister
articles.summary: Heavy Industries Minister HD Kumaraswamy stated that India's electric mobility transition must focus on urban and rural markets through interoperable charging. Industry players including Ather Energy and Hero MotoCorp launched the LEAF platform to develop cost-effective solutions and seamless charging experiences at scale.

articles.headline: PM Modi inaugurates, lays foundation for projects worth Rs 4,000 crore
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated and laid the foundation stone for development projects worth over Rs 4,000 crore in Gujarat. The projects include water supply, sewerage, and infrastructure development.

articles.headline: City unveils plan for smart traffic management system
articles.summary: A metropolitan city has unveiled a comprehensive plan for implementing a smart traffic management system. The aim is to reduce congestion and improve traffic flow.

articles.headline: New solar power plant inaugurated in rural India
articles.summary: A large-scale solar power plant has been inaugurated in a rural area of India, aiming to provide clean and sustainable energy to the local population.

articles.headline: Urban planners discuss sustainable city development strategies
articles.summary: Urban planners and policymakers convened to discuss strategies for sustainable city development. Key topics included green infrastructure and resilient urban planning.

articles.headline: Indian Railways modernizes tracks with advanced technology
articles.summary: Indian Railways is implementing advanced technology to modernize its track infrastructure. This initiative aims to improve safety, speed, and efficiency of train operations.

articles.headline: Gram Panchayats process ₹3 lakh crore in online funds
articles.summary: Over 2.5 lakh gram panchayats have collectively processed more than ₹3 lakh crore in virtual payments through the eGramSwaraj platform since April 2019. In FY26 alone, ₹53,342 crore was transferred. The digital system ensures direct payments to vendors with a transparent audit trail, effectively replacing the previous cash-based expenditure regime in rural governance.

articles.headline: Akasa Air adds six Boeing planes to fleet in first quarter
articles.summary: Akasa Air took delivery of six Boeing 737 MAX 8-200 aircraft in Q1 2026, bringing its total fleet size to 37. The airline, which launched in 2022, has a total order for 226 Boeing aircraft. The remaining 189 planes are expected to join the fleet over the next six years as the airline continues its expansion in the domestic and international markets.

articles.headline: Centre clears New Mangalore Port berth redevelopment proposal
articles.summary: The government has approved the redevelopment of berth number 9 at New Mangalore Port for handling liquid bulk cargo. The project, valued at approximately ₹28,307 crore, will be executed under a public-private partnership model. It involves dismantling legacy infrastructure to create a modern facility for cargo management.

articles.headline: IndiGo launches direct flight service between Shanghai and Kolkata
articles.summary: IndiGo has launched a daily direct flight service between Shanghai and Kolkata to boost connectivity between India and China. The new service was inaugurated by the Indian Consulate General in Shanghai. Additionally, Air China plans to launch a direct flight between Beijing and New Delhi starting April 21 to meet growing regional travel demand.

articles.headline: BDA invites tenders for 73km business corridor first phase in Bengaluru
