articles.summary: The Bangalore Development Authority has floated tenders for the first 23km stretch of the Peripheral Ring Road, now renamed the Bengaluru Business Corridor. The project, estimated at ₹3,348 crore for the first phase, aims to connect Tumakuru Road and Hosur Road. Land acquisition for the 73km corridor is underway after years of legal and administrative delays.

articles.headline: National Highway toll rates to increase by five to fifteen rupees
articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) announced a hike in toll rates for several highways across the country effective April 1. In Bengaluru, tolls on the International Airport Road and other major corridors will see an increase of ₹5 to ₹15 per journey. The revision is part of the annual inflation-linked adjustment process for highway maintenance and development.

articles.headline: Generative AI reshapes coding education and entry-level software jobs in India
articles.summary: The rise of AI is lowering barriers to software development, allowing non-technical students to build tools while forcing engineering colleges to rethink curricula. While AI-related skills are the most in-demand on platforms like Coursera, entry-level hiring for low-complexity coding roles is dropping. The industry is shifting from 'doing' to 'directing' using AI agents like GitHub Copilot.

articles.headline: R. Ashoka criticizes Karnataka's new SSLC third language grading system
articles.summary: Leader of Opposition R. Ashoka criticized the Karnataka government’s decision to remove pass/fail weightage for Hindi as a third language in SSLC exams. Ashoka alleged the move was politically motivated and would create confusion for students mid-exam. He argued that the change could disadvantage Kannada students seeking national service careers in Hindi-speaking states.

articles.headline: Government revokes NIT Kurukshetra director's powers and forms review committee
articles.summary: The Union Ministry of Education withdrew the administrative and financial powers of NIT Kurukshetra director BV Ramana Reddy. A three-member committee led by Anil Sahasrabudhe will review the institute's work and progress. The move follows unspecified concerns regarding the institute's functioning, with the committee expected to submit a report within one month.

articles.headline: Forty student suicides reported in Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas since 2021
articles.summary: The Union government told the Lok Sabha that 40 student suicides were reported in Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas (JNVs) over the last five years. JNVs are a system of central residential schools for talented students from rural areas. The figures cover the period between 2021 and early 2026, highlighting ongoing concerns about student mental health in high-pressure academic environments.

articles.headline: AI chatbot used in exams raises concerns about academic integrity
articles.summary: The increasing use of AI chatbots in academic settings has sparked concerns regarding academic integrity and assessment methods in educational institutions.

articles.headline: Government proposes new framework for digital education
articles.summary: The government has proposed a new framework to enhance digital education across schools and universities. The policy focuses on accessibility and quality of online learning.

articles.headline: India’s climate transition model serves as example for the Global South
articles.summary: India's updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) aim for a 47% reduction in emissions intensity by 2035. The strategy balances green transition with economic growth, proving that decarbonization need not sacrifice development. By focusing on people-centric initiatives like the LiFE movement, India is redefining climate leadership for developing nations with limited finance access.

articles.headline: Delhi set to record warmest March in four years despite cool end
articles.summary: Delhi is on track to record its warmest March in four years with an average monthly maximum of 32.5°C. This is 2.6°C above the long-period average. Despite a series of western disturbances bringing cooler temperatures late in the month, high temperatures in the first half drove the average up. The last time March was warmer was in 2022.

articles.headline: Karnataka faces drinking water shortages in 207 taluks this summer
articles.summary: A report by the State Level Weather Watch Committee warns that 207 taluks in Karnataka are likely to face drinking water shortages this summer. El Niño conditions are expected to persist, potentially leading to dry spells during the monsoon. Authorities have already deployed tankers to 246 villages, while heatwave conditions are predicted for north-interior Karnataka.

articles.headline: Activists condemn painting of elephant pink for photoshoot in Jaipur
articles.summary: A Russian photographer, Julia Buruleva, faced social media backlash and criticism from animal rights activists for painting an elephant pink during a photoshoot at Jaipur's Amer Palace. Activists stated that any paints or colors are harmful to an elephant's sensitive skin. The incident has renewed calls for better protection of captive animals used in tourism and entertainment.

articles.headline: New study reveals increased microplastic pollution in Indian rivers
articles.summary: A recent study highlights a significant increase in microplastic pollution in major Indian rivers. The findings underscore the urgent need for better waste management and policy.

articles.headline: Delhi govt announces new policy to curb stubble burning
articles.summary: The Delhi government has introduced a new policy aimed at reducing stubble burning by providing farmers with alternatives and incentives. This is a measure to combat air pollution.

articles.headline: Local government launches initiative for urban green spaces
articles.summary: A local municipal body has launched an initiative to develop and expand urban green spaces within the city, aiming to improve environmental quality and public well-being.

articles.headline: Community celebrates successful river clean-up drive
articles.summary: Residents of a locality celebrated the success of a community-driven river clean-up initiative. The campaign significantly improved the water quality and surrounding environment.

articles.headline: Conservation efforts yield positive results for endangered species
articles.summary: Conservation efforts have shown positive results for an endangered species in a specific region. The population has seen a notable increase due to dedicated protection measures.

articles.headline: Start-up develops eco-friendly fertilizer from waste
articles.summary: An agricultural start-up has developed an eco-friendly fertilizer using industrial waste. This innovation offers a sustainable solution for soil enrichment.

articles.headline: New study highlights impact of climate change on agriculture
articles.summary: A recent study has highlighted the significant impact of climate change on agricultural productivity in India. The findings emphasize the need for adaptation strategies.

articles.headline: Opium cultivators suspected in tiger killing in Madhya Pradesh forests
articles.summary: A forest department probe into the death of a tiger in Chhindwara has pointed toward local opium cultivators. Investigators believe a bull was poisoned to protect crops from livestock, which the tiger then consumed. This follows reports of illegal opium farming in the region. Three suspects have been arrested as officials trace the movement of the radio-collared animal.

articles.headline: Sloth bear enters Shivamogga airport after 50km trek into town
articles.summary: An eight-year-old male sloth bear was captured at the Sogane airport in Shivamogga after wandering through residential areas for 24 hours. Forest officials tranquilized the animal, which had traveled nearly 50km from the Bhadra Wildlife Sanctuary. The bear was later relocated to an observation cell at Shivamogga zoo for health monitoring before its release.

articles.headline: Managing persistent hypertension through emotional health and dietary lifestyle changes
articles.summary: Persistent hypertension requires more than just medication; focusing on emotional health and the 'invisibles' that spike heart rates is critical. For those diagnosed young, shifts toward low-salt diets and ending habits like alcohol consumption are essential. The condition is often linked to emotional triggers, making mindfulness and stress management as important as clinical treatments.

articles.headline: Hooghly district officer withdraws poll duty order for 49 hospital doctors
articles.summary: A district election officer in West Bengal reversed an order requiring 49 specialist doctors from a state hospital to join poll duty. The reversal came after medical authorities protested that the mass deployment would cripple OPD and emergency services. While doctors were exempted, administrative staff from the hospital remain assigned to election duties.

articles.headline: Medical device makers seek measures to ease working capital distress
articles.summary: The Association of Indian Medical Device Industry (Aimed) has urged the Finance Ministry to fast-track GST refunds and provide temporary customs duty rebates. Manufacturers reported a 50% rise in plastics costs and doubling of gas prices due to the West Asia war, straining liquidity for makers of essential medical disposables.

articles.headline: Scientists develop new method for early detection of disease
articles.summary: Researchers have developed an innovative method for the early detection of a prevalent disease. This breakthrough offers potential for improved patient outcomes and treatment strategies.

articles.headline: Health ministry launches campaign for adolescent mental health
articles.summary: The Union Health Ministry has launched a nationwide campaign focusing on adolescent mental health. The initiative aims to raise awareness and provide support resources.

articles.headline: Apollo Hospitals partners with air ambulance services for critical care
articles.summary: Apollo Hospitals has entered into MoUs with national and international air ambulance providers to improve patient access to advanced critical care. Coordinated through Apollo's 1066 emergency network, the initiative aims to create a connected pathway for clinical coordination and hospital preparedness during medical transport across India and abroad.

articles.headline: RKC plank technique offers specialized training for core strength and stability
articles.summary: The Russian Kettlebell Club (RKC) plank, developed by Soviet special forces trainer Pavel Tsatsouline, uses total body tension and breathwork to build core strength. Unlike basic planks, it requires engaging glutes, quads, and hamstrings to maximize effectiveness. Consistent 10-second holds can significantly improve lower back health and lift performance for fitness enthusiasts.

articles.headline: Australian-born Ryan Williams eligible to debut for Indian football team
articles.summary: 32-year-old winger Ryan Williams is set to debut for India against Hong Kong in the AFC Asian Cup 2027 Qualifiers. Born in Australia, Williams renounced his citizenship last year to represent India, following in the footsteps of his Indian-born mother. He joins a rare list of players to debut for two different international teams during their careers.

articles.headline: Indian chess player Divya Deshmukh draws with R. Vaishali in Candidates
articles.summary: 19-year-old Divya Deshmukh missed a chance to take the lead in the Women's Candidates tournament after settling for a draw against fellow Indian R. Vaishali. In the open section, R. Praggnanandhaa also played out a draw with Chinese Grandmaster Wei Yi. All matches on Day 2 of the tournament in Cyprus ended in draws as players employed cautious opening strategies.

articles.headline: AIFF denies harassment allegations made by women's committee head
articles.summary: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) rejected harassment claims made by its women's committee head, Valanka Alemao, against president Kalyan Chaubey. The AIFF countered that Alemao was pressuring the federation to include her family's club, Churchill Brothers, in the ISL through exceptional means. Alemao alleged she was heckled and intimidated during a recent meeting.

articles.headline: Italy faces Bosnia in crucial World Cup qualification playoff
articles.summary: Italy faces Bosnia and Herzegovina in Zenica on Tuesday for a high-stakes World Cup qualification playoff. Coach Gennaro Gattuso described the match as a major hurdle as Italy attempts to avoid missing a third consecutive tournament. The match is clouded by controversy over Italian players' celebrations following Bosnia's earlier victory over Wales.

articles.headline: Rajasthan Royals defeat Chennai Super Kings by eight wickets in Guwahati
articles.summary: Rajasthan Royals crushed Chennai Super Kings by eight wickets after bowling them out for 127. 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed a 17-ball 52 to lead the chase. Jofra Archer and Nandre Burger took two wickets each as CSK struggled without MS Dhoni. The win keeps the Royals at the top of the table as the IPL season progresses.

articles.headline: Shardul Thakur's three-wicket haul helps Mumbai Indians beat KKR
articles.summary: In his first match at the Wankhede for Mumbai Indians, Shardul Thakur took three crucial wickets to defeat Kolkata Knight Riders. Thakur utilized clever slower balls and knuckle balls on a flat deck to dismiss key batters including Finn Allen and Ajinkya Rahane. MI captain Hardik Pandya praised Thakur's "cricket smarts" in breaking high-scoring patterns.

articles.headline: Cricket Australia clarifies Cameron Green's back injury status for IPL
articles.summary: Cricket Australia confirmed that KKR was fully informed about Cameron Green's lower back injury, which prevents him from bowling temporarily. Green is expected to resume bowling in 10-12 days after rebuilding his load in India. The clarification follows comments from KKR captain Ajinkya Rahane suggesting the team was unaware of why the all-rounder could not bowl.

articles.headline: National Rifle Association to build ammunition testing facilities in India
articles.summary: The NRAI plans to set up ammunition testing centers at the Karni Singh Range in Delhi, with additional facilities in Pune and Bhopal. The move aims to reduce dependence on European manufacturers and bypass procurement delays caused by the Ukraine war. Shooters currently face waits of up to 11 months for imported ammunition, affecting their training and competition readiness.

articles.headline: Indian athletes prepare for upcoming international sports event
articles.summary: Indian athletes are undergoing rigorous training and preparation for an upcoming international sports competition. They aim to achieve medals and represent the nation with pride.

articles.headline: Vaibhav Suryavanshi slams third fastest fifty in IPL history at fifteen
articles.summary: 15-year-old Vaibhav Suryavanshi made history by scoring the third-fastest fifty in IPL history, reaching the mark in just 15 balls. Playing for the Rajasthan Royals against Chennai Super Kings, the teenager hit five sixes and four boundaries in his 52-run knock. His performance led the Royals to a dominant eight-wicket victory, overshadowing veteran stars in the opening week.

articles.headline: Amit Shah declares India Naxal-free in Lok Sabha address
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah declared India "Naxal-free" during a Lok Sabha speech, noting that 4,839 Maoists surrendered and 706 were killed in three years. He criticized previous governments and a 2011 Supreme Court judgment for failing to stop the insurgency. The government had set a March 31, 2026, deadline to eradicate left-wing extremism and claims the goal has been achieved.

articles.headline: Nitish Kumar and Nitin Nabin resign from Bihar state legislature
articles.summary: Bihar CM Nitish Kumar and BJP chief Nitin Nabin resigned from the state’s legislative council and assembly respectively after being elected to the Rajya Sabha. Constitutional rules require members elected to both Parliament and a state legislature to resign from one within 14 days. Kumar remains CM and will take his Rajya Sabha seat tentatively on April 10.

articles.headline: Bangladesh Foreign Minister to visit India for fuel and water-sharing talks
articles.summary: Bangladesh Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman will visit New Delhi next week to discuss the renewal of the Ganga Waters Treaty and requests for additional fuel supplies. This is the first visit by a senior member of the Tarique Rahman-led government. Discussions will focus on energy crises caused by the West Asian war and hydrological assessments for the 30-year-old water pact.

articles.headline: TVK chief Vijay declares assets worth 603 crore rupees in poll affidavit
articles.summary: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam chief Vijay declared assets totaling ₹603.20 crore in his election affidavit. The disclosure includes ₹213 crore in bank deposits and immovable properties like agricultural land and commercial buildings worth ₹198.62 crore. Vijay is contesting the upcoming Tamil Nadu assembly elections from the Perambur and Tiruchirappalli East constituencies.

articles.headline: Siddaramaiah campaigns for Congress in Bagalkote by-election
articles.summary: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah campaigned in Bagalkote for candidate Umesh Meti, framing the by-election as an endorsement of the state’s welfare guarantee schemes. Meanwhile, BJP state president B.Y. Vijayendra accused the Congress of neglecting the district and using money power. The election, scheduled for April 9, follows the death of senior leader H.Y. Meti.

articles.headline: Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan accepts opposition challenge for public debate
articles.summary: Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan has accepted a challenge from Leader of Opposition V.D. Satheesan for a public debate ahead of the April 9 elections. The debate follows a row over the LDF government's housing construction record. Vijayan shared a "report card" of his administration's achievements and invited the opposition to discuss those specific points.

articles.headline: Top Maoist leader and eight others surrender to Andhra Pradesh police
articles.summary: Chelluri Narayana Rao, a high-ranking Maoist central committee member with a ₹25 lakh reward, surrendered to Andhra Pradesh police alongside eight other operatives. The group cited a lack of local support and declining recruitment as reasons for leaving the movement. The surrender occurred just before the Centre's deadline to end left-wing extremism in India.

articles.headline: Pinarayi Vijayan asks PM Modi to withdraw proposed FCRA amendment bill
articles.summary: Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan wrote to Prime Minister Modi seeking the withdrawal of the FCRA Amendment Bill, 2026. He argued the bill grants authorities sweeping powers to take over assets of charitable and educational institutions for minor technical omissions. Vijayan warned that such arbitrary powers could adversely affect selfless service organizations in the state.

articles.headline: MK Stalin and actor Vijay file nominations for Tamil Nadu polls
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu CM M.K. Stalin and TVK chief Vijay filed their nomination papers for the April 23 assembly elections. Stalin is seeking a fourth term from Kolathur and projected a victory for the DMK. Vijay, making his electoral debut from Perambur, attacked the government over unemployment and law and order. Other candidates turned up in bullock carts or submitted deposits in coins.

articles.headline: PM Modi criticizes Congress and DMK in Puducherry virtual address
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi virtually addressed booth-level workers in Puducherry, accusing the Congress and DMK of prioritizing "family first" over the public. He claimed the NDA represents a "Puducherry first" approach and highlighted that the region's crime rate against women is lower than the national average. He urged workers to use data to counter opposition narratives.

articles.headline: BRS seeks removal of Telangana Minister over illegal mining allegations
articles.summary: A BRS delegation led by K.T. Rama Rao submitted a memorandum to the Telangana governor demanding the removal of Minister Ponguleti Srinivas Reddy. The party alleged his construction firm engaged in illegal mining and land grabbing in restricted reservoir catchment areas. BRS called for a probe by a sitting High Court judge into the alleged losses to the state exchequer.

articles.headline: PM Modi asserts illegal immigration is a matter of national security
articles.summary: In an address to BJP workers in Assam, Prime Minister Narendra Modi declared illegal immigration a threat to national security and the state's identity. He accused the Congress of protecting infiltrators for vote banks and causing land encroachments. Modi urged party members to document negative impacts of immigration and reach out to beneficiaries of government schemes.

articles.headline: Abhishek Manu Singhvi questions status of CEC removal notice in Parliament
articles.summary: Congress MP Abhishek Manu Singhvi questioned why a notice for the removal of Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar hasn't been addressed 19 days after submission. The notice, signed by 193 MPs, accuses the CEC of being subservient to the executive. Under the Constitution, a CEC can only be removed in the same manner as a Supreme Court judge, requiring a two-thirds majority.

articles.headline: Election Commission transfers four senior officers in West Bengal reshuffle
articles.summary: The ECI transferred four of its own senior officers in West Bengal, including a deputy chief electoral officer and two additional secretaries. This follows a larger reshuffle involving 83 block development officers and 184 police officers across the state. The moves come amid ongoing litigation in the Calcutta High Court challenging recent series of official transfers.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee declares national ambitions after upcoming West Bengal elections
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee declared that her party will work to "capture Delhi" after the upcoming state elections. Speaking at a rally, she focused on Bengali identity and accused the Centre of using electoral roll revisions to control the state. Banerjee framed the contest as a fight against the BJP's attempt to undermine the state's political and cultural autonomy.

articles.headline: Tripura CPI(M) demands FIR against BJP minister for offensive speech
articles.summary: The CPI(M) in Tripura has asked the state election commission to file an FIR against BJP minister Bikash Debbarma. The party alleged he made offensive remarks regarding the transfer of votes between the TIPRA Motha and BJP during a campaign rally. The opposition also sought a ban on Debbarma’s further participation in election campaigning for violating conduct norms.

articles.headline: Seventy-eight candidates file nominations for first phase of Bengal elections
articles.summary: A total of 78 nominations were filed on the first day of the West Bengal assembly elections across 152 constituencies. Notable filings include Leader of the Opposition Suvendu Adhikari in Nandigram. April 6 is the final date for filing papers for the first phase, which follows massive reshuffles of administrative and police personnel by the Election Commission.

articles.headline: TMC alleges attempts to enroll outsiders on West Bengal electoral rolls
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress general secretary Abhishek Banerjee raised concerns over attempts to enroll "outsiders" as voters in West Bengal. The party claimed to have reports of large-scale Form 6 applications from individuals with no connection to the state. TMC argued these entries are intended to manipulate the electoral process ahead of the high-stakes assembly elections.

articles.headline: BJP MP K. Laxman alleges misuse of OBC quotas in opposition-ruled states
articles.summary: BJP MP K. Laxman alleged in the Rajya Sabha that states like Telangana, West Bengal, and Kerala are including all Muslims in OBC quotas. He claimed this practice contravenes reservation policies that do not provide religion-based quotas. The remarks led to a walkout by opposition members, while BJP President JP Nadda criticized the opposition for engaging in appeasement politics.

articles.headline: BJD protests BJP MP Nishikant Dubey's remarks on late Biju Patnaik
articles.summary: Biju Janata Dal members walked out of the Rajya Sabha to protest BJP MP Nishikant Dubey's claims that former Odisha CM Biju Patnaik had links to the CIA. Current CM Naveen Patnaik described the comments as "outrageous" and suggested Dubey needs medical attention. Even BJP's Jay Panda defended the late leader, calling the aspersions "ludicrous" and unacceptable.

articles.headline: India Naval-free: Shah Says Naxalism Nearing End in LS
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah informed the Lok Sabha that Naxal-affected areas have shrunk significantly, with only 12 police stations across the country now reporting such activity. He claimed nearly 12 crore people have been liberated from deprivation. Shah reiterated the government's goal to eliminate Left-Wing Extremism by March 2026 through focused development and security measures.

articles.headline: DMK Manifesto Bets Big on MSMEs and Investment Push
articles.summary: The DMK released its manifesto for the upcoming elections, pledging to attract ₹18 lakh crore in industrial investment if voted to power for a second term. Key focus areas include supporting MSMEs, promoting the electronics manufacturing sector, and investing ₹50,000 crore into semiconductor production. The party aims to replicate successful industrial models to boost Tamil Nadu's GDP.

articles.headline: MeitY Moves to Regulate Online User-posted News
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and IT has proposed amendments to the IT Rules, 2021, to bring online user-generated news content under regulatory oversight. The move would subject social media influencers and citizen journalists sharing news to the same scrutiny and ethics code as digital news publishers. The draft aims to curb misinformation but has raised concerns about free speech.

articles.headline: DMK MP says AIADMK has surrendered to BJP in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: DMK MP Thamizhachi Thangapandian stated that the upcoming polls are a fight between the Dravidian engine and the BJP, alleging the AIADMK has compromised Tamil interests. She highlighted the importance of Chennai's second airport and suggested TVK founder Vijay's dual constituency candidacy shows a lack of confidence.

articles.headline: UDF advantage narrows as LDF rebuilds support base in Kerala
articles.summary: Analysts suggest the United Democratic Front (UDF) is losing its early electoral advantage in Kerala to 'narrative drift.' While the ruling LDF has stabilized its base through organizational discipline, the UDF is accused of focusing on intrigue rather than presenting a governance roadmap. The outcome may depend on whether the Opposition can mount a credible critique of the LDF's record.

articles.headline: Amit Shah says talks ongoing with only active top Maoist member
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah stated that the government is in communication with the only remaining active member of the CPI (Maoist) central committee. He claimed other members have either surrendered or been neutralized. Shah noted that the Naxal movement is nearing its end in Jharkhand and Chhattisgarh, attributing the progress to focused security operations.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi accuses Kerala CPM of stealing Sabarimala gold funds
articles.summary: During an election rally in Kerala, Congress leader Rahul Gandhi accused the ruling CPM of misappropriating funds and gold belonging to the Sabarimala temple. He alleged that the state government is shielding corrupt officials and has failed to maintain transparency in temple administration. Gandhi also promised a central probe into the matter if the Congress coalition wins the assembly polls.

articles.headline: PM Modi flags illegal migrant influx into Assam as national threat
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed a rally in Assam, describing the influx of illegal migrants as a 'matter of life and death' for the state's identity. He urged the public to support BJP's efforts to implement the National Register of Citizens and secure the borders. Modi emphasized that the protection of indigenous culture and land is the government's top priority.

articles.headline: Indian Navy receives delivery of two warships and survey vessel
articles.summary: In a major boost to maritime security, the Indian Navy took delivery of two new warships—a stealth frigate and an anti-submarine warfare vessel—along with a survey ship. The vessels, built by Indian shipyards, were commissioned in a single day in Kolkata. This delivery marks a significant step in the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative for indigenous defense production.

articles.headline: Census 2027 to include state-specific SC and ST lists in forms
articles.summary: The 2027 Digital Census will provide state-specific lists of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes to assist respondents. An FAQ for the self-enumeration portal clarifies that couples in stable live-in relationships will be treated as married. The first phase, focusing on houselisting and housing, begins on April 1, 2026. Individual data will remain confidential under the Census Act.

articles.headline: Navy technician arrested in Visakhapatnam for killing and dismembering girlfriend
articles.summary: A 35-year-old Indian Navy technician, Chintada Ravindra, was arrested for allegedly murdering his 31-year-old girlfriend, Mounika, in Visakhapatnam. Police state the accused dismembered the body, stored parts in a refrigerator, and burned others to destroy evidence. The technician surrendered to police on Monday morning following the crime on Sunday.

articles.headline: Retired Brigadier killed by stray bullet following nightclub brawl in Dehradun
articles.summary: Retired Brigadier MK Joshi, 70, was killed by a stray bullet while out on a morning walk in Dehradun. The bullet was fired during a high-speed car chase between two groups following a brawl at a local nightclub. Police have arrested four people from both groups and registered a murder case under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.

articles.headline: Bootlegger arrested for using camels to smuggle liquor into Delhi
articles.summary: Delhi Police arrested 48-year-old Vinod Bhandana for attempting to smuggle 39 cartons of illicit liquor from Faridabad on camelback. This is the second time in six months Bhandana has been caught using camels as mules. Police seized two camels, Kalli and Bhuri, who were previously released to the accused from an animal shelter just 25 days ago.

articles.headline: Sukma residents embrace mobile towers and voting as Naxal influence wanes
articles.summary: In Chhattisgarh's Sukma, residents of once-impregnable Maoist strongholds like Tekalgurium are applying for voter IDs for the first time. The installation of mobile towers and security camps has brought connectivity and government presence to the region. While many welcome the end of forced food donations to rebels, some express anxiety over the impact of modernity on tribal culture.

articles.headline: Private bus plunges off bridge in Shivamogga injuring thirty-six people
articles.summary: Thirty-six people were injured after a private bus plunged off a bridge into a stream in Veerapura village, Shivamogga district. The bus hit a side barrier and overturned while traveling from Channagiri to Bhadravati. Police have registered a case against the driver for losing control of the vehicle. All victims were taken to a nearby hospital for treatment.

articles.headline: Police rescue woman chained by family for a decade in Belagavi
articles.summary: Belagavi police rescued a woman who was allegedly chained inside her home by her parents for ten years. The family believed her mental illness was the result of black magic and sought help from a godman instead of medical professionals. The woman, who began showing symptoms while in college, was restrained because the family feared her aggressive behavior.

articles.headline: Hyderabad man arrested for sexually assaulting speech-impaired woman
articles.summary: A 35-year-old man was arrested in Hyderabad for allegedly sexually assaulting a speech-impaired and physically disabled relative. The incident occurred on March 28 in Attapur when the victim was alone at home. Police registered a case under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) after the victim identified the suspect, who allegedly gagged and assaulted her.

articles.headline: Uttar Pradesh man arrested in Kochi for sophisticated ATM card thefts
articles.summary: Kochi police arrested a 34-year-old man from Aligarh for a series of thefts targeting two-wheelers. The suspect used duplicate keys to open luggage boxes, stole ATM cards, and withdrew funds from victims' bank accounts. The arrest followed a complaint from a victim in Thrissur, leading police to uncover the sophisticated theft operation.

articles.headline: Election Commission launches home voting for elderly and disabled electors
articles.summary: The ECI has introduced home voting for electors over 85 and persons with disabilities in Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry. Over 237,000 voters have been approved for the postal ballot facility. The initiative aims to improve accessibility and participation in the assembly polls. The process is currently underway and will continue through April 5.

articles.headline: Manipur government orders closure of eleven relief camps as situation improves
articles.summary: The Manipur government ordered the closure of 11 relief camps in Kakching district, citing improved law and order. The move facilitates the return of thousands of internally displaced persons (IDPs) to their homes in areas like Sugnu and Serou. The government aims to resettle 10,000 families and has sanctioned 7,000 houses for reconstruction following ethnic violence that began in 2023.

articles.headline: Alleged Lashkar-e-Taiba recruiter arrested by Delhi Police in Ghazipur
articles.summary: Delhi Police arrested 43-year-old Shabir Ahmed Lone, an alleged Lashkar-e-Taiba recruiter, from east Delhi. Lone is accused of leading a gang that defaced metro stations with pro-Pakistan and pro-terror posters glorifying Burhan Wani. Police said he entered India via Nepal after operating from Bangladesh and had a prior UAPA arrest in 2007 for possessing AK-47 rifles and grenades.

articles.headline: Pak Terror Module Busted; Two Held in Joint Operation
articles.summary: Jammu and Kashmir Police, in collaboration with Punjab Police, busted a Pakistan-linked terror module and detained two nationals in Malerkotla. Identified as Abu Huraira and Usman, the duo was allegedly planning an attack in the Christian belt. The arrests follow a major breakthrough in tracing cross-border infiltrators using forged Indian identity documents to reside in the country.

articles.headline: Digital Census First Phase to Kick Off in 11 States
articles.summary: India's first digital census will commence on April 1, 2026, starting with 11 states and Union Territories. The exercise will be conducted in two phases, with the second phase covering the remaining regions. Citizens can opt for self-enumeration through a portal. The 2027 census will include 34 questions focused on demographic, socio-economic, and housing data across the nation.

articles.headline: Heatwave conditions persist in several parts of India
articles.summary: Several regions across India continue to experience severe heatwave conditions. Authorities have issued advisories for public safety and advised precautions against heatstroke.

articles.headline: India to host G20 Tourism Working Group meeting in Srinagar
articles.summary: Srinagar is set to host the G20 Tourism Working Group meeting, focusing on tourism cooperation and promotion. The event aims to showcase local tourism potential.

articles.headline: IMD predicts normal monsoon rainfall for India
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasts normal monsoon rainfall across the country. This brings relief to agricultural sectors anticipating water for the Kharif season.

articles.headline: New agricultural technique promises higher crop yields
articles.summary: A novel agricultural technique, developed by local researchers, is showing promise for significantly increasing crop yields. Farmers are being trained in its implementation.

articles.headline: Panic buying sparks spike in fertilizer sales across India
articles.summary: Farmers are stockpiling fertilizers as government data shows March sales have already surpassed estimated demand for the full month. Urea sales hit 20.21 lakh tonnes against a 14.96 lakh tonne estimate. High demand was noted in Telangana, Madhya Pradesh, and Maharashtra. The government has ordered physical stock verification at the district level to curb hoarding.

articles.headline: Centre opens emergency window to sell kerosene for cooking
articles.summary: The government has notified a 60-day emergency window allowing select petrol pumps to sell PDS kerosene for cooking and lighting. The move aims to assist migrant laborers unable to refill LPG cylinders due to supply disruptions caused by the West Asia conflict. An ad hoc allocation of 48,000 kilolitres of kerosene has been made to States and Union Territories.

articles.headline: Navy official arrested for allegedly killing lover and dismembering body
articles.summary: A 30-year-old Navy petty officer was arrested in Visakhapatnam for the murder of a woman he was in a relationship with. Police report that Chintada Ravindra allegedly strangled the victim in his rented flat before cutting the body into three parts and storing them in a refrigerator. The crime was uncovered after the victim's family reported her missing.

articles.headline: Digital Census 2027 to begin from April 1 across India
articles.summary: The Census 2027 operations will commence on April 1, marking the first time data will be collected through a mobile app. The Registrar General of India announced that the digital transition will ensure faster processing and accurate data. The first phase includes house listing and housing census, with new questions aimed at capturing socio-economic shifts and migration patterns.

articles.headline: In a First, CERN Scientists Achieve Antimatter Transport
articles.summary: Scientists at CERN have successfully transported 92 antiprotons across their Geneva campus using a portable cryogenic trap, a first in physics history. The antiprotons, composed of antimatter, were moved on a truck to a laboratory for ultra-precise measurements. The breakthrough paves the way for deeper studies into why the universe is dominated by matter rather than antimatter.

articles.headline: Government boosts funding for renewable energy research
articles.summary: The government has announced increased funding for research and development in renewable energy technologies. This initiative aims to accelerate the transition to sustainable energy sources.

articles.headline: Study identifies new species of insect in Western Ghats
articles.summary: A recent biodiversity survey in the Western Ghats has identified a new species of insect. This discovery highlights the region's rich and unique ecosystem.

articles.headline: Research identifies potential treatment for rare genetic disorder
articles.summary: Scientists have identified a potential new treatment for a rare genetic disorder. This discovery offers hope for patients suffering from the condition.

articles.headline: Astronomers discover distant exoplanet with water signature
articles.summary: Astronomers have discovered a distant exoplanet showing potential signs of water. The finding is a significant step in the search for habitable worlds beyond our solar system.

articles.headline: India to offer 21 oil and gas blocks in latest bidding round
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Hydrocarbons announced that 21 blocks will be offered in the 11th round of the Open Acreage Licensing Policy (OALP-XI). The round includes 12 on-land areas, four shallow water blocks, one deepsea block, and four ultra-deep sea blocks. The initiative is part of India's strategy to increase domestic energy prospecting amid global supply disruptions.

articles.headline: Fertilizer stocks to meet 39 million tonne kharif demand by May
articles.summary: Government officials expect to bridge the gap in fertilizer stocks during April and May to meet the 39 million tonne requirement for the upcoming kharif season. Currently, India has 18 million tonnes in stock. Despite West Asia conflict vulnerabilities, the department of fertilizers stated that strategic planning will ensure adequate supplies for farmers starting in June.

articles.headline: India fiscal deficit reaches 80 per cent of revised estimates
articles.summary: India's fiscal deficit touched ₹12.5 lakh crore in the April-February period, representing over 80% of the revised estimate for the full year. This is lower than the 86% recorded in the same period last year. Economists expect the government to meet its 4.4% GDP deficit target, though rising crude prices and geopolitical tensions remain significant risks to the budget math.

articles.headline: Industrial Output Growth Rises 5.2% in February
articles.summary: India's industrial production grew 5.2% year-on-year in February, supported by healthy manufacturing and electricity output. However, consumer non-durables remained a weak spot, contracting by 0.6%. Economists expect growth to moderate in March as the West Asia conflict begins to impact global trade and supply chains, with ICRA projecting a 3-4% expansion for the full month.

articles.headline: Indian equities end FY26 with weakest showing in six years
articles.summary: Markets ended the final session of FY26 sharply lower, with the Nifty 50 falling 2.14% to a one-year low. The Sensex dropped 1,636 points. The decline was fueled by the five-week-long US-Iran conflict and crude oil prices staying above $100 per barrel. Financial and banking indices bore the brunt of the sell-off following new RBI forex restrictions.

articles.headline: Rupee hits new low of 94.83 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee closed at a record low of 94.83 per US dollar on the final day of FY26, swinging 170 paise intra-day. Despite RBI measures to curb speculation by capping banks' net open positions, rising crude prices and month-end importer demand weighed heavily on the currency. The rupee has depreciated approximately 11% throughout the 2025-26 fiscal year.

articles.headline: Government leaves small savings interest rates unchanged for Q1
articles.summary: The government has kept interest rates for small savings schemes, including PPF and NSC, unchanged for the eighth straight quarter. The rates for the first quarter of FY27 (April 1 to June 30, 2026) will remain the same as those notified for the previous quarter. The decision follows a notification from the Finance Ministry issued on Monday.

articles.headline: Finance Minister states rupee remains resilient compared to peers
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman told the Lok Sabha that the Indian rupee is performing well relative to other emerging economy currencies despite hitting record lows. She noted that the South Korean won, Thai baht, and Philippine peso have depreciated more than the rupee's 4.1% decline during the current conflict. RBI is monitoring the market to curb excess volatility.

articles.headline: ICRA projects India GDP growth to slow to 6.5% in FY27
articles.summary: Rating agency ICRA estimates India's GDP growth will moderate to 6.5% in the next fiscal year from 7.6% currently. The slowdown is attributed to elevated energy prices and supply concerns stemming from the West Asia conflict. ICRA also expects the current account deficit to widen to 1.7% of GDP, assuming an average crude oil price of $85 per barrel.

articles.headline: CCI hikes cotton floor prices by ₹1,300 per candy
articles.summary: The Cotton Corporation of India increased floor prices for the 2025-26 crop by ₹1,300 per candy to align with firming global markets and strong domestic demand. Raw cotton prices have moved above MSP levels in major markets. Analysts expect cotton acreage to rise by 20% in the upcoming season as farmers shift away from water-intensive crops in anticipation of El Nino.

articles.headline: Mustard output projected to grow 3.6% on higher area
articles.summary: The Solvent Extractors’ Association estimated India's rapeseed-mustard production at 119.4 lakh tonnes for 2025-26, up from 115.2 lt. The growth is driven by a 1.9% increase in acreage and improved agronomic practices. Rajasthan continues to lead production with 53.9 lt, followed by Uttar Pradesh, while some states like Assam reported slightly lower yields.

articles.headline: Government considers EPFO and ESIC benefits for unorganised sector
articles.summary: The Ministry of Labour is mulling a plan to bring 32 crore unorganised, gig, and platform workers under the ambit of EPFO and ESIC. The model would likely involve voluntary worker contributions paired with government subsidies. The move aims to fulfill the mandate of the four Labour Codes and includes the launch of free doorstep services for pension schemes.

articles.headline: Draft IT rules propose wider scrutiny of user-generated news content
articles.summary: The IT Ministry has proposed amendments to the IT Rules, 2021, to expand oversight over online content. The new rules seek to regulate user-generated news content posted by individuals, such as influencers, who are not formally classified as publishers. The Internet Freedom Foundation has called for the withdrawal of the draft, citing concerns over constitutional protections.

articles.headline: Minister warns electronics firms over missed manufacturing targets
articles.summary: IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw has warned electronics manufacturers that fund disbursements under the ECMS scheme may be halted if targets for in-house design and Six Sigma standards are not met. Giving the industry a 15-day deadline to share progress plans, the Minister emphasized that companies failing to invest in design will eventually be 'weeded out' of the ecosystem.

articles.headline: India's sovereign AI models find early takers in health and education
articles.summary: Sovereign AI models under development by Fractal Analytics and Tech Mahindra are receiving early interest from hospitals and educational institutions. These models, slated for 2026 completion, offer customized solutions with localized linguistic relevance. While early feedback is positive, analysts caution that broad enterprise adoption faces hurdles in production readiness and triaging.

articles.headline: CoinDCX launches ₹100-crore initiative for digital cyber safety
articles.summary: Crypto exchange CoinDCX has committed ₹100 crore to build the Digital Suraksha Network (DSN) to enhance cyber safety in India's digital finance ecosystem. The initiative includes a 24/7 WhatsApp helpline to verify links and platforms in real time. The project aims to create a shared infrastructure between regulators and platforms to protect citizens from digital fraud.

articles.headline: Vembu Technologies to invest ₹100 crore in India expansion
articles.summary: Vembu Technologies plans to invest ₹100 crore over the next 3-5 years to scale its backup and disaster recovery solutions in India. CEO Sekar Vembu highlighted the growth potential driven by India's data center expansion. The company aims to compete with multinationals by offering localized support and matching global product standards for its BDRShield platform.

articles.headline: Vodafone Idea to expand 5G services to 90 more cities
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea (Vi) announced plans to rollout 5G services in 90 new cities by May 2026, bringing its total coverage to 133 cities. The expansion will cover 15 circles using AI-powered self-optimising networks. Vi has partnered with Nokia, Ericsson, and Samsung for the rollout, prioritizing high-data consumption centers and industrial corridors.

articles.headline: Bharti Airtel data center arm Nxtra raises one billion dollars
articles.summary: Nxtra, the data center unit of Bharti Airtel, has raised $1 billion to fund its expansion across India. The investment will be used to build new hyperscale data centers in major cities to meet the rising demand for cloud services and 5G infrastructure. Nxtra aims to double its capacity within the next three years, targeting a leading position in the Asian digital infrastructure market.

articles.headline: Venu Srinivasan returns as Sundaram Clayton chairman amid board reversal
articles.summary: In a rare corporate turn, Sundaram Clayton rescinded the resignation of company secretary P.D. Dev Kishan just 72 hours after accepting it. Chairman emeritus Venu Srinivasan has officially taken over as chairman, replacing R. Gopalan. The moves follow concerns raised by managing director Lakshmi Venu regarding corporate governance and reporting structures within the TVS Group firm.

articles.headline: Japan’s JFE Steel invests ₹7,875 crore in JSW Kalinga venture
articles.summary: JFE Steel Corp. has acquired a 25% stake in JSW Kalinga Steel Ltd with an investment of ₹7,875 crore. This is the first tranche of a planned ₹15,750 crore joint venture. The partnership includes the integrated steel plant in Odisha and Bhushan Power and Steel Ltd. The investment highlights continued foreign interest in India's expanding steel manufacturing capacity.

articles.headline: XED Executive Development withdraws GIFT City IPO due to war and delays
articles.summary: XED Executive Development has withdrawn its $12 million IPO on the NSE International Exchange at GIFT City. The company cited delays in customer verification and weak market sentiments caused by the US-Israeli war on Iran. The withdrawal is a setback for efforts to build GIFT City into a global capital markets hub competing with Singapore and Dubai. The IPO was only 5% subscribed.

articles.headline: Jubilant FoodWorks to exit Dunkin’ franchise agreement by year end
articles.summary: Jubilant FoodWorks will not renew its agreement to operate Dunkin' stores in India after the contract expires on December 31. The coffee-and-doughnut chain struggled to scale, contributing less than 1% of Jubilant's revenue and recording a ₹19.1 crore loss in FY25. Jubilant will now focus on its Domino’s pizza business and newer brands like Popeyes fried chicken.

articles.headline: CMPDIL shares debut with 10% discount on Indian stock exchanges
articles.summary: Shares of Central Mine Planning and Design Institute Ltd (CMPDIL), a subsidiary of Coal India, ended their first day of trading 10.43% below their issue price of ₹172. The stock listed at a discount on both the BSE and NSE, with the company's market valuation settling at ₹10,999.17 crore. The ₹1,842-crore IPO was entirely an offer for sale by parent company Coal India.

articles.headline: Wheels India appoints Srivats Ram as new Chairman
articles.summary: Wheels India has appointed its current Managing Director, Srivats Ram, as the Chairman of the company effective March 31, 2026. This follows the resignation of S Ram, the outgoing Chairman and Non-Executive Director. The leadership transition comes as the company continues its operations in the automotive components sector.

articles.headline: Auto sector faces disruption in Q1 as growth slows
articles.summary: Crisil warns of a disrupted start to FY27 for the Indian automobile sector due to supply-side stress, rising energy costs, and production bottlenecks. Passenger vehicle volume growth is expected to moderate to 3-5%, while electric vehicles remain a bright spot. Structural shifts, such as the dominance of SUVs and declining hatchback shares, continue to reshape the market.

articles.headline: West Asia conflict increases stress for Indian textile exporters
articles.summary: The Indian apparel and textile sector is facing fresh headwinds from the West Asia crisis, with yarn prices rising by 30-40%. Rising costs for coal, chemicals, and man-made fibers are squeezing margins. Exporters report that consignments are being held up in warehouses as West Asian clients face payment difficulties and local industries face severe LPG shortages.

articles.headline: Zerodha and others limit brokerage hikes ahead of STT change
articles.summary: Ahead of the higher Securities Transaction Tax (STT) on derivatives starting April 1, Zerodha has restricted its proposed brokerage hike to only those clients with large margin shortfalls. Other brokers like Kotak Securities are avoiding broad hikes to protect trading volumes. The industry had previously urged the government to roll back the tax increase to maintain liquidity.

articles.headline: NSE initiates IPO process by inviting expressions of interest
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange of India (NSE) has formally begun its listing process by inviting shareholders to indicate their interest in an offer for sale (OFS). Shareholders have until April 27 to respond. The exchange recently appointed 20 merchant bankers, including Kotak Mahindra Capital and Citigroup, to manage the long-pending public offering.

articles.headline: Zetwerk eyes ₹500 crore pre-IPO funding round
articles.summary: Contract manufacturing firm Zetwerk is looking to raise nearly ₹500 crore in a pre-IPO round. Currently valued at ₹10,500 crore, the company has appointed bankers including Kotak Mahindra and Goldman Sachs for the process. Zetwerk has raised over $859 million in equity funding to date and is preparing for a future public listing.

articles.headline: Nazara Technologies raises ₹500 crore for global expansion
articles.summary: Nazara Technologies raised ₹500 crore via a preferential issue of warrants to five investors, including Plutus Investment and Riambel Capital. The capital will fund acquisition strategies and the development of AI-enabled gaming businesses. This follows the company's recent acquisitions of BestPlay and Bluetile as it seeks to scale its global sports media and gaming presence.

articles.headline: Pioneer Fil-Med files draft papers for ₹500-crore IPO
articles.summary: Railway component manufacturer Pioneer Fil-Med has filed draft documents with SEBI for a ₹500 crore initial public offering. The IPO includes a fresh issue of ₹250 crore and an offer for sale of ₹250 crore by existing shareholders. Proceeds are intended for debt repayment, meeting lease obligations for experience stores and warehouses, and general corporate purposes.

articles.headline: PropShare Celestia to launch India's first SM REIT IPO
articles.summary: PropShare Celestia will open a ₹244.65 crore IPO on April 10, marking India's first registered Small and Medium REIT. The offer consists of fresh units with a minimum investment of ₹10 lakh. Proceeds will be used to acquire seven floors of a Grade A+ commercial building in Ahmedabad. The IPO will conclude on April 16.

articles.headline: Moody’s applies to establish branch in Gift IFSC
articles.summary: Moody’s Investors Service Singapore has filed an application to open a branch in Gujarat's Gift IFSC. This follows S&P Global Ratings' recent entry. The presence of major global credit rating agencies is expected to strengthen India's offshore financial hub by supporting debt listings, sovereign ratings, and trade finance instruments within the IFSC framework.

articles.headline: Insurance sector to adopt Ind AS reporting from April 1
articles.summary: IRDAI has introduced the Indian Accounting Standards (Ind AS) based reporting framework for all insurers starting April 1. Companies will maintain parallel reporting for two years to assess the impact and stabilize processes. The move aims to enhance the consistency and global comparability of financial reports across life, general, and standalone health insurance sectors.

articles.headline: Summer heat and cricket season drive double-digit beer demand
articles.summary: Indian beermakers expect double-digit volume growth this season due to high temperatures and major cricket tournaments. United Breweries and SOM Group report a shift toward premium brands and larger pack formats for home consumption. However, the West Asia conflict is driving up costs for glass and aluminum, putting pressure on profit margins despite rising offtake.

articles.headline: Vedanta moves Supreme Court to challenge Adani takeover of Jaiprakash Associates
articles.summary: Vedanta Ltd has escalated its legal battle against the Adani Group’s ₹14,543 crore resolution plan for Jaiprakash Associates. Vedanta claims its higher offer was ignored and the bidding process lacked fairness. The petition follows a refusal by the NCLAT to stay the plan's implementation. The dispute centers on maximizing value for creditors who have admitted claims of approximately ₹60,637 crore.

articles.headline: Rajasthan High Court raises concerns over Transgender Persons Amendment Bill
articles.summary: The Rajasthan High Court observed that the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, risks removing the right to self-perceived gender identity. The court noted that making identity contingent on administrative certification is a departure from Supreme Court precedents. It urged the state to prioritize self-identification in its affirmative action policies.

articles.headline: Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code amendments passed by Lok Sabha
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the IBC (Amendment) Bill, 2025, which requires the Committee of Creditors to record detailed reasons for selecting successful resolution applicants. The move aims to increase transparency and reduce litigation. The Bill also introduces provisions for group and cross-border insolvency and replaces fast-track processes with a new creditor-initiated framework.

articles.headline: Donald Trump warns Iran of escalation as US troops arrive
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has repeated threats to destroy Iranian energy assets if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened immediately. Approximately 3,500 sailors and Marines have arrived in the region on an amphibious assault ship. The IMF warned that the ongoing conflict could trigger a global asymmetric economic shock, leading to higher prices and slower growth.

articles.headline: India to offer 21 blocks for oil and gas prospecting
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Hydrocarbons announced India will offer 21 blocks for oil and natural gas prospecting in the 11th OALP bidding round. The acreage covers over 80,000 square kilometers across onland, shallow water, and deepsea blocks. The OALP-XI round aims to boost domestic production, with awards based on revenue share and proposed work programmes.

articles.headline: Premier Energies commissions 5.6 GW solar module facility in Telangana
articles.summary: Premier Energies has launched a new 5.6 GW solar module manufacturing plant in Seetharampur, Telangana, creating an estimated 2,000 jobs. The facility brings the company's total module capacity to 11.1 GW. The investment aligns with India's renewable energy goals and aims to enhance domestic supply chain resilience for high-efficiency solar solutions.

articles.headline: NHAI raises ₹28,307 crore through highway asset monetisation
articles.summary: NHAI has raised ₹28,307 crore through InvIT and Toll-Operate-Transfer (TOT) models, nearing its annual target of ₹30,000 crore. Key deals included the ₹6,366.98 crore award of a 310 km highway stretch to NHIT Western Projects. The monetisation program aims to leverage operational assets to fund new infrastructure development across several states.

articles.headline: Health insurance complaints rise 14% over previous fiscal year
articles.summary: Health insurance complaints on the Bima Bharosa portal reached 73,729 in 11 months, surpassing the total for FY25. Common grievances include claim rejections due to exclusion clauses or outpatient categorization. IRDAI has mandated that insurers provide specific reasons for repudiation and resolved that no claim be rejected without approval from a management committee.

articles.headline: Jannik Sinner wins Miami Open title after defeating Jiri Lehecka
articles.summary: World No. 2 Jannik Sinner won the Miami Open title by defeating Jiri Lehecka 6-4, 6-4 in the final. This victory marks Sinner's third title of the 2026 season and his first Miami Open crown. The Italian became the first man to complete the 'Sunshine Double' in 2026 without dropping a set throughout the tournament, solidifying his rank at the top of the ATP standings.

articles.headline: FCRA Changes to Benefit RSS, says Rahul Gandhi
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi criticized proposed changes to the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA), alleging they are designed to benefit the RSS and its affiliates while crippling other secular NGOs. Speaking in Kerala, he claimed the move targets organizations working in the Christian belt and accused the BJP of using policy to consolidate its own organizational interests.

articles.headline: Actor Vijay kickstarts Tamil Nadu Assembly election campaign
articles.summary: Actor-turned-politician Vijay, founder of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), formally launched his election campaign after filing nominations for the Perambur and Tiruchirapalli seats. Vijay attacked the ruling DMK for family-driven politics. His election affidavit revealed movable and immovable assets worth approximately ₹624.58 crore and a annual income of ₹184.5 crore.

articles.headline: PM Modi claims double-engine government brought peace to Assam
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated that Assam has transitioned from instability to peace in the last decade thanks to 12 peace agreements signed by the BJP government. Addressing booth-level workers, Modi claimed the Congress previously misled the public with agreements that existed only on paper, while the current administration has delivered honest results for the youth.

articles.headline: Church raises concerns over FCRA amendments ahead of Kerala polls
articles.summary: Bishops of the Syro-Malabar Church voiced concerns that proposed amendments to the FCRA are 'unjust and unethical.' The Church fears that tighter license renewal norms will disrupt charitable and educational institutions that rely on foreign funds. The issue has become a key concern for the Christian community in Kerala ahead of the upcoming Assembly elections.

articles.headline: West Bengal roll revision sparks political row over name deletions
articles.summary: The Election Commission's Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls in West Bengal has led to a net deletion of over 61 lakh voters. While the BJP claims the exercise removes 'infiltrators,' the Trinamool Congress has called it 'silent invisible rigging.' Several citizens reported their names were missing despite resubmitting required documentation to local officers.

articles.headline: BJP approaches EC alleging voter intimidation in West Bengal
articles.summary: A BJP delegation filed a petition with the Election Commission alleging that West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee and TMC leaders are intimidating voters. The party requested that Banerjee be debarred from campaigning and called for the deployment of additional central forces and IAS/IPS observers to ensure peaceful polling in sensitive areas of the State.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi claims PM Modi is compromised by Donald Trump
articles.summary: Leader of the Opposition Rahul Gandhi alleged that PM Narendra Modi is controlled by US President Donald Trump, and that Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan is in turn controlled by Modi. Speaking at a rally in Adoor, Gandhi claimed the BJP and LDF are working together in Kerala to oppose the Congress despite public instances of corruption and misgovernance.

articles.headline: Shipping Directorate extends validity of crew certificates for seafarers
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Shipping has extended the validity of crew competency and safety certificates by three months. The measure provides relief to approximately 485 Indian seafarers currently stranded on 18 vessels in the Western Persian Gulf region. The extension addresses compliance and insurance risks arising from logistical constraints during the ongoing conflict.

articles.headline: Registrar General urges accurate data for Census 2027 rollout
articles.summary: Registrar General Mritunjay Kumar Narayan has urged citizens to provide correct data for Census 2027, starting April 1. For the first time, people can fill information digitally through a dedicated website. The census will be conducted in phases, with the first phase focusing on housing and the second on socio-economic parameters and a caste census.

articles.headline: Kashmir farmers face crisis as fuel sales in cans are halted
articles.summary: Fuel stations in Kashmir have stopped selling petrol and diesel in cans and bottles following panic buying triggered by shortage fears. Thousands of orchardists depend on this fuel to run chemical spray pumps for apple trees. The ban comes at a critical pre-bloom stage for the region's 22 lakh tonne annual apple production, potentially leading to crop losses.

articles.headline: Israel Weapon Industries delivers first batch of Negev machine guns
articles.summary: The Indian Army received the first 2,000 Negev light machine guns from Israel Weapon Industries (IWI). Over 50% of the units were manufactured in India through a joint venture with Adani. The total contract for 41,000 units is planned over five years. IWI is also participating in bids to supply 4,500 sniper rifles and close-quarter battle carbines to the armed forces.
